discord.ext.commands.flag(_\*_, _name\=..._, _aliases\=..._, _default\=..._, _max\_args\=..._, _override\=..._, _converter\=..._, _description\=..._, _positional\=..._)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.flag "Permalink to this definition")

Override default functionality and parameters of the underlying [`FlagConverter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.FlagConverter "discord.ext.commands.FlagConverter")
 class attributes.

Parameters

*   **name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) – The flag name. If not given, defaults to the attribute name.
    
*   **aliases** (List\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")\
    \]) – Aliases to the flag name. If not given no aliases are set.
    
*   **default** (_Any_) – The default parameter. This could be either a value or a callable that takes [`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
     as its sole parameter. If not given then it defaults to the default value given to the attribute.
    
*   **max\_args** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) – The maximum number of arguments the flag can accept. A negative value indicates an unlimited amount of arguments. The default value depends on the annotation given.
    
*   **override** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) – Whether multiple given values overrides the previous value. The default value depends on the annotation given.
    
*   **converter** (_Any_) – The converter to use for this flag. This replaces the annotation at runtime which is transparent to type checkers.
    
*   **description** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) – The description of the flag.
    
*   **positional** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) –
    
    Whether the flag is positional or not. There can only be one positional flag.
    
    New in version 2.1.

.

Parameters

**ctx** ([`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
) – The invocation context that is used to get the default argument.

discord.ext.commands.parameter(_\\\*_, _converter=..._, _default=..._, _description=..._, _displayed\_default=..._, _displayed\_name=..._)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.parameter "Permalink to this definition")

A way to assign custom metadata for a [`Command`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command "discord.ext.commands.Command")
's parameter.

New in version 2.0.

Examples

A custom default can be used to have late binding behaviour.

content\_copy

@bot.command()
async def wave(ctx, to: discord.User \= commands.parameter(default\=lambda ctx: ctx.author)):
    await ctx.send(f'Hello {to.mention} :wave:')

Parameters

*   **converter** (_Any_) – The converter to use for this parameter, this replaces the annotation at runtime which is transparent to type checkers.
    
*   **default** (_Any_) – The default value for the parameter, if this is a [callable](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-callable "(in Python v3.14)")
     or a [_coroutine_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-task.html#coroutine)
     it is called with a positional [`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
     argument.
    
*   **description** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) – The description of this parameter.
    
*   **displayed\_default** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) – The displayed default in [`Command.signature`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.signature "discord.ext.commands.Command.signature")
    .
    
*   **displayed\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    ) –
    
    The name that is displayed to the user.
    
    New in version 2.1.
    

discord.ext.commands.param(_\*_, _converter_, _default_, _description_, _displayed\_default_, _displayed\_name_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.param "Permalink to this definition")

param(\*, converter=…, default=…, description=…, displayed\_default=…, displayed\_name=…)

An alias for [`parameter()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.parameter "discord.ext.commands.parameter")
.

New in version 2.0.

discord.ext.commands.Author[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.discord.ext.commands.Author "Permalink to this definition")

A default [`Parameter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Parameter "discord.ext.commands.Parameter")
 which returns the [`author`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.author "discord.ext.commands.Context.author")
 for this context.

New in version 2.0.

discord.ext.commands.CurrentChannel[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.discord.ext.commands.CurrentChannel "Permalink to this definition")

A default [`Parameter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Parameter "discord.ext.commands.Parameter")
 which returns the [`channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.channel "discord.ext.commands.Context.channel")
 for this context.

New in version 2.0.

discord.ext.commands.CurrentGuild[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.discord.ext.commands.CurrentGuild "Permalink to this definition")

A default [`Parameter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Parameter "discord.ext.commands.Parameter")
 which returns the [`guild`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.guild "discord.ext.commands.Context.guild")
 for this context. This will never be `None`. If the command is called in a DM context then [`NoPrivateMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.NoPrivateMessage "discord.ext.commands.NoPrivateMessage")
 is raised to the error handlers.

New in version 2.0.

Exceptions[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#exceptions "Permalink to this headline")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.CommandError(_message\=None_, _\*args_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandError "Permalink to this definition")

The base exception type for all command related errors.

This inherits from [`discord.DiscordException`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.DiscordException "discord.DiscordException")
.

This exception and exceptions inherited from it are handled in a special way as they are caught and passed into a special event from [`Bot`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot "discord.ext.commands.Bot")
, [`on_command_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.discord.ext.commands.on_command_error "discord.discord.ext.commands.on_command_error")
.

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ConversionError(_converter_, _original_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ConversionError "Permalink to this definition")

Exception raised when a Converter class raises non-CommandError.

This inherits from [`CommandError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandError "discord.ext.commands.CommandError")
.

converter[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ConversionError.converter "Permalink to this definition")

The converter that failed.

Type

[`discord.ext.commands.Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")

original[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ConversionError.original "Permalink to this definition")

The original exception that was raised. You can also get this via the `__cause__` attribute.

Type

[`Exception`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#Exception "(in Python v3.14)")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.MissingRequiredArgument(_param_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.MissingRequiredArgument "Permalink to this definition")

Exception raised when parsing a command and a parameter that is required is not encountered.

This inherits from [`UserInputError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.UserInputError "discord.ext.commands.UserInputError")

param[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.MissingRequiredArgument.param "Permalink to this definition")

The argument that is missing.

Type

[`Parameter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Parameter "discord.ext.commands.Parameter")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.MissingRequiredAttachment(_param_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.MissingRequiredAttachment "Permalink to this definition")

Exception raised when parsing a command and a parameter that requires an attachment is not given.

This inherits from [`UserInputError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.UserInputError "discord.ext.commands.UserInputError")

New in version 2.0.

param[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.MissingRequiredAttachment.param "Permalink to this definition")

The argument that is missing an attachment.

Type

[`Parameter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Parameter "discord.ext.commands.Parameter")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ArgumentParsingError(_message\=None_, _\*args_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ArgumentParsingError "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when the parser fails to parse a user’s input.

This inherits from [`UserInputError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.UserInputError "discord.ext.commands.UserInputError")
.

There are child classes that implement more granular parsing errors for i18n purposes.

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.UnexpectedQuoteError(_quote_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.UnexpectedQuoteError "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when the parser encounters a quote mark inside a non-quoted string.

This inherits from [`ArgumentParsingError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ArgumentParsingError "discord.ext.commands.ArgumentParsingError")
.

quote[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.UnexpectedQuoteError.quote "Permalink to this definition")

The quote mark that was found inside the non-quoted string.

Type

[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.InvalidEndOfQuotedStringError(_char_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.InvalidEndOfQuotedStringError "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when a space is expected after the closing quote in a string but a different character is found.

.

char[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.InvalidEndOfQuotedStringError.char "Permalink to this definition")

The character found instead of the expected string.

Type

[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ExpectedClosingQuoteError(_close\_quote_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExpectedClosingQuoteError "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when a quote character is expected but not found.

.

close\_quote[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExpectedClosingQuoteError.close_quote "Permalink to this definition")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.BadArgument(_message\=None_, _\*args_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.BadArgument "Permalink to this definition")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.BadUnionArgument(_param_, _converters_, _errors_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.BadUnionArgument "Permalink to this definition")

errors[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.BadUnionArgument.errors "Permalink to this definition")

\]

errors[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.BadLiteralArgument.errors "Permalink to this definition")

\]

 have failed.

.

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.CommandNotFound(_message\=None_, _\*args_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandNotFound "Permalink to this definition")

Exception raised when a command is attempted to be invoked but no command under that name is found.

This is not raised for invalid subcommands, rather just the initial main command that is attempted to be invoked.

This inherits from [`CommandError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandError "discord.ext.commands.CommandError")
.

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.TooManyArguments(_message\=None_, _\*args_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.TooManyArguments "Permalink to this definition")

 attribute was not set to `True`.

.

 decorator.

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError(_message\=None_, _\*args_, _name_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError "Permalink to this definition")

Base exception for extension related errors.

This inherits from [`DiscordException`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.DiscordException "discord.DiscordException")
.

name[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError.name "Permalink to this definition")

The extension that had an error.

Type

[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ExtensionAlreadyLoaded(_name_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionAlreadyLoaded "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when an extension has already been loaded.

This inherits from [`ExtensionError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotLoaded(_name_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotLoaded "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when an extension was not loaded.

This inherits from [`ExtensionError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.NoEntryPointError(_name_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.NoEntryPointError "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when an extension does not have a `setup` entry point function.

This inherits from [`ExtensionError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ExtensionFailed(_name_, _original_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionFailed "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when an extension failed to load during execution of the module or `setup` entry point.

This inherits from [`ExtensionError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError")

name[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionFailed.name "Permalink to this definition")

The extension that had the error.

Type

[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

original[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionFailed.original "Permalink to this definition")

The original exception that was raised. You can also get this via the `__cause__` attribute.

Type

[`Exception`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#Exception "(in Python v3.14)")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound(_name_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when an extension is not found.

This inherits from [`ExtensionError`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionError")

Changed in version 1.3: Made the `original` attribute always None.

name[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound.name "Permalink to this definition")

The extension that had the error.

Type

[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

_exception_ discord.ext.commands.CommandRegistrationError(_name_, _\*_, _alias\_conflict\=False_)[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandRegistrationError "Permalink to this definition")

An exception raised when the command can’t be added because the name is already taken by a different command.

This inherits from [`discord.ClientException`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ClientException "discord.ClientException")

New in version 1.4.

name[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandRegistrationError.name "Permalink to this definition")

The command name that had the error.

Type

[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")

alias\_conflict[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandRegistrationError.alias_conflict "Permalink to this definition")

Whether the name that conflicts is an alias of the command we try to add.

Type

[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.14)")

# Migrating to v2.0

View Documentation For discord discord.ext.commands discord.ext.tasks

 search

settings

Migrating to v2.0[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-to-v2-0 "Permalink to this headline")

====================================================================================================================================

Compared to v1.0, v2.0 mostly has breaking changes related to better developer experience and API coverage. While the changes aren’t as massive to require an entire rewrite, there are still many changes that need to be accounted for.

This is the first version to have version guarantees apply.

Below is a **non-exhaustive** list of changes.

Python Version Change[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#python-version-change "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to ease development, maintain security updates, and use newer features **v2.0 drops support for Python 3.7 and earlier**.

asyncio Event Loop Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#asyncio-event-loop-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Python 3.7 introduced a new helper function [`asyncio.run()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-runner.html#asyncio.run "(in Python v3.11)")
 which automatically creates and destroys the asynchronous event loop.

In order to support this, the way discord.py-self handles the [`asyncio`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html#module-asyncio "(in Python v3.11)")
 event loop has changed.

This allows you to rather than using [`Client.run()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.run "discord.Client.run")
 create your own asynchronous loop to setup other asynchronous code as needed.

Quick example:

content\_copy

client \= discord.Client()

async def main():
    \# do other async things
    await my\_async\_function()

    \# start the client
    async with client:
        await client.start(TOKEN)

asyncio.run(main())

A new [`setup_hook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.setup_hook "discord.Client.setup_hook")
 method has also been added to the [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 class. This method is called after login but before connecting to the discord gateway.

It is intended to be used to setup various bot features in an asynchronous context.

[`setup_hook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.setup_hook "discord.Client.setup_hook")
 can be defined by subclassing the [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 class.

Quick example:

content\_copy

class MyClient(discord.Client):
    async def setup\_hook(self):
        print('This is asynchronous!')

client \= MyClient()
client.run(TOKEN)

With this change, constructor of [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 no longer accepts `connector` and `loop` parameters.

In parallel with this change, changes were made to loading and unloading of commands extension extensions and cogs, see [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
 for more information.

Abstract Base Classes Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#abstract-base-classes-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Abstract Base Classes](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord-api-abcs)
 that inherited from [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.11)")
 now inherit from [`typing.Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.11)")
.

This results in a change of the base metaclass used by these classes but this should generally be completely transparent to the user.

All of the classes are either [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.11)")
 protocols or explicitly inherited from and as such usage with [`isinstance()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#isinstance "(in Python v3.11)")
 and [`issubclass()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#issubclass "(in Python v3.11)")
 is not affected.

The following have been changed to [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.11)")
 [`Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.11)")
s:

*   [`abc.Snowflake`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Snowflake "discord.abc.Snowflake")
    
*   [`abc.User`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.User "discord.abc.User")
    

The following have been changed to subclass [`Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.11)")
:

*   [`abc.GuildChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel "discord.abc.GuildChannel")
    
*   [`abc.Connectable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Connectable "discord.abc.Connectable")
    

The following have been changed to use the default metaclass instead of [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.11)")
:

*   [`abc.Messageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable "discord.abc.Messageable")
    
*   [`abc.PrivateChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.PrivateChannel "discord.abc.PrivateChannel")
    

`datetime` Objects Are Now UTC-Aware[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#datetime-objects-are-now-utc-aware "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All usage of naive [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 objects in the library has been replaced with aware objects using UTC timezone. Methods that accepted naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 objects now also accept timezone-aware objects. To keep behavior inline with [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
’s methods, this library’s methods now assume that naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 objects are local time (note that some of the methods may not accept naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
, such exceptions are listed below).

Because naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 objects are treated by many of its methods as local times, the previous behavior was more likely to result in programming errors with their usage.

To ease the migration [`utils.utcnow()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.utcnow "discord.utils.utcnow")
 helper function has been added.

Warning

Using [`datetime.datetime.utcnow()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.utcnow "(in Python v3.11)")
 can be problematic since it returns a naive UTC `datetime` object.

Quick example:

content\_copy

\# before
week\_ago \= datetime.datetime.utcnow() \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
if member.created\_at \> week\_ago:
    print(f'Member account {member} was created less than a week ago!')

\# after
\# The new helper function can be used here:
week\_ago \= discord.utils.utcnow() \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
\# ...or the equivalent result can be achieved with datetime.datetime.now():
week\_ago \= datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc) \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
if member.created\_at \> week\_ago:
    print(f'Member account {member} was created less than a week ago!')

The following have been changed from naive to aware [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 objects in UTC:

*   [`AuditLogEntry.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.created_at "discord.AuditLogEntry.created_at")
     attribute

     attribute

     event

     event

The following now accept aware [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 and assume that if the passed [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 is naive, it is a local time:

*   [`abc.Messageable.history()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.history "discord.abc.Messageable.history")
     method
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guilds()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guilds "discord.Client.fetch_guilds")
     method
    
*   [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
     method
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_members "discord.Guild.fetch_members")
     method
    
*   [`TextChannel.purge()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.purge "discord.TextChannel.purge")
     method
    
*   [`Embed`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Embed "discord.Embed")
     constructor
    
*   [`Embed.timestamp`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Embed.timestamp "discord.Embed.timestamp")
     property setter
    
*   [`utils.sleep_until()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.sleep_until "discord.utils.sleep_until")
     function
    
*   [`utils.time_snowflake()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.time_snowflake "discord.utils.time_snowflake")
     function
    

Currently, there’s only one place in this library that doesn’t accept naive [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.11)")
 objects:

*   `timed_out_until` parameter in [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
    > This has been done to prevent users from mistakenly applying incorrect timeouts to guild members.
    

Major Webhook Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#major-webhook-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Webhook support has been rewritten to work better with typings and rate limits.

As a result, synchronous functionality has been split to separate classes.

Quick example for asynchronous webhooks:

content\_copy

\# before
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    webhook \= discord.Webhook.from\_url('url-here', adapter\=discord.AsyncWebhookAdapter(session))
    await webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

\# after
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    webhook \= discord.Webhook.from\_url('url-here', session\=session)
    await webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

Quick example for synchronous webhooks:

content\_copy

\# before
webhook \= discord.Webhook.partial(123456, 'token-here', adapter\=discord.RequestsWebhookAdapter())
webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

\# after
webhook \= discord.SyncWebhook.partial(123456, 'token-here')
webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   Synchronous functionality of [`Webhook`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook "discord.Webhook")
     and [`WebhookMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage "discord.WebhookMessage")
     has been split to [`SyncWebhook`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.SyncWebhook "discord.SyncWebhook")
     and [`SyncWebhookMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.SyncWebhookMessage "discord.SyncWebhookMessage")
    .
    
*   `WebhookAdapter` class has been removed and the interfaces based on it (`AsyncWebhookAdapter` and `RequestsWebhookAdapter`) are now considered implementation detail and should not be depended on.
    
*   `execute` alias for [`Webhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.send "discord.Webhook.send")
    /[`SyncWebhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.SyncWebhook.send "discord.SyncWebhook.send")
     has been removed.
    

Asset Redesign and Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#asset-redesign-and-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 object now encompasses all of the methods and attributes related to a CDN asset.

This means that all models with asset-related attribute and methods have been transformed to use this new design. As an example, here’s how these changes look for [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
 (of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 type):

*   `Guild.icon` (of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
     type) has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    .
    
*   `Guild.is_icon_animated` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.is_animated`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.is_animated "discord.Asset.is_animated")
    .
    
*   `Guild.icon_url` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
    .
    
*   `Guild.icon_url_as` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    .
    
    > *   Helper methods [`Asset.with_size()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.with_size "discord.Asset.with_size")
    >     , [`Asset.with_format()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.with_format "discord.Asset.with_format")
    >     , and [`Asset.with_static_format()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.with_static_format "discord.Asset.with_static_format")
    >      have also been added.
    >     
    

In addition to this, [`Emoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji "discord.Emoji")
 and [`PartialEmoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.PartialEmoji "discord.PartialEmoji")
 now also share an interface similar to [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
’s:

*   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
     is now of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
     type.
    
*   `Emoji.url_as` has been removed.
    
*   `Emoji.url.read` has been replaced with [`Emoji.read()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.read "discord.Emoji.read")
    .
    
*   `Emoji.url.save` has been replaced with [`Emoji.save()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.save "discord.Emoji.save")
    .
    

[`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 now always represent an actually existing CDN asset. This means that:

*   `str(x)` on an [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can no longer return an empty string.
    
*   `bool(x)` on an [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can no longer return `False`.
    
*   Attributes containing an optional [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can now be `None`.
    

The following were affected by this change:

*   [`AuditLogDiff`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff "discord.AuditLogDiff")
    
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.avatar "discord.AuditLogDiff.avatar")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.icon "discord.AuditLogDiff.icon")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.splash "discord.AuditLogDiff.splash")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    
*   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
    
    > *   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
    >      is now of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url_as` has been removed.
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url.read` (replaced by [`Emoji.read()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.read "discord.Emoji.read")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url.save` (replaced by [`Emoji.save()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.save "discord.Emoji.save")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`GroupChannel.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.GroupChannel.icon "discord.GroupChannel.icon")
    
    > *   `GroupChannel.icon` (replaced by [`GroupChannel.icon.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `GroupChannel.icon_url` (replaced by [`GroupChannel.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.GroupChannel.icon "discord.GroupChannel.icon")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `GroupChannel.icon_url_as` (replaced by [`GroupChannel.icon.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Guild.banner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.banner "discord.Guild.banner")
    
    > *   `Guild.banner` (replaced by [`Guild.banner.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Guild.banner_url` (replaced by [`Guild.banner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.banner "discord.Guild.banner")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `Guild.banner_url_as` (replaced by [`Guild.banner.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Guild.discovery_splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.discovery_splash "discord.Guild.discovery_splash")
    
    > *   `Guild.discovery_splash` (replaced by [`Guild.discovery_splash.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Guild.discovery_splash_url` (replaced by [`Guild.discovery_splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.discovery_splash "discord.Guild.discovery_splash")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `Guild.discovery_splash_url_as` (replaced by [`Guild.discovery_splash.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")

    >     )

    >     >

    >     )

Thread Support[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#thread-support "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

v2.0 has been updated to use a newer API gateway version which supports threads and as a result of this had to make few breaking changes. Most notably messages sent in guilds can, in addition to a [`TextChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel "discord.TextChannel")
, be sent in a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
.

The main differences between text channels and threads are:

*   Threads do not have their own permissions, they inherit the permissions of their parent channel.
    
    > *   This means that threads do not have these attributes:
    >     
    >     > *   `changed_roles`
    >     >     
    >     > *   `overwrites`
    >     >     
    >     > *   `permissions_synced`
    >     >     
    >     
    > 
    > Note
    > 
    > Text channels have a few dedicated permissions for threads:
    > 
    > *   [`Permissions.manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.create_public_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.create_public_threads "discord.Permissions.create_public_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.create_private_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.create_private_threads "discord.Permissions.create_private_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own NSFW status, they inherit it from their parent channel.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have an `nsfw` attribute.
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own topic.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have a `topic` attribute.
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own position in the channel list.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have a `position` attribute.
    >     
    
*   [`Thread.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.created_at "discord.Thread.created_at")
     of threads created before 10 January 2022 is `None`.
    
*   [`Thread.members`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.members "discord.Thread.members")
     is of type List\[[`ThreadMember`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ThreadMember "discord.ThreadMember")\
    \] rather than List\[[`Member`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member "discord.Member")\
    \]
    
    > *   Most of the time, this data is not provided and a call to [`Thread.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.fetch_members "discord.Thread.fetch_members")
    >      to fetch the partial objects is needed.
    >     
    

For convenience, [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
 has a set of properties and methods that return the information about the parent channel:

*   [`Thread.category`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.category "discord.Thread.category")
    
*   [`Thread.category_id`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.category_id "discord.Thread.category_id")
    
*   [`Thread.is_news()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.is_news "discord.Thread.is_news")
    
*   [`Thread.is_nsfw()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.is_nsfw "discord.Thread.is_nsfw")
    
*   [`Thread.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.permissions_for "discord.Thread.permissions_for")
    
    > *   Note that this outputs the permissions of the parent channel and you might need to check for different permissions when trying to determine if a member can do something.
    >     
    >     Here are some notable examples:
    >     
    >     *   A guild member can send messages in a text channel if they have [`send_messages`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages "discord.Permissions.send_messages")
    >          permission in it.
    >         
    >         A guild member can send messages in a public thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             .
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can send messages in a private thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They’re either already a member of the thread or have a [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             .
    >             
    >         
    >     *   A guild member can edit a text channel if they have [`manage_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_channels "discord.Permissions.manage_channels")
    >          permission in it.
    >         
    >         A guild member can edit a thread if they have [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >          permission in its parent channel.
    >         
    >         Note
    >         
    >         A thread’s [`owner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.owner "discord.Thread.owner")
    >          can archive a (not-locked) thread and edit its [`name`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.name "discord.Thread.name")
    >          and [`auto_archive_duration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.auto_archive_duration "discord.Thread.auto_archive_duration")
    >          without [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >          permission.
    >         
    >     *   A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a text channel if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in it.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in it or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a public thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in its parent channel or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`archived`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.archived "discord.Thread.archived")
    >             . Note that the guild member can unarchive a thread (if it’s not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             ) to react to a message.
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a private thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in its parent channel or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         *   They’re either already a member of the thread or have a [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`archived`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.archived "discord.Thread.archived")
    >             . Note that the guild member can unarchive a thread (if it’s not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             ) to react to a message.
    >             
    >         
    

The following changes have been made:

*   [`Message.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.channel "discord.Message.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Message.channel_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.channel_mentions "discord.Message.channel_mentions")
     list may now contain a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`AuditLogEntry.target`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.target "discord.AuditLogEntry.target")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`PartialMessage.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.PartialMessage.channel "discord.PartialMessage.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.get_channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel "discord.Guild.get_channel")
     does not return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    s.
    
    > *   If you’re looking to get a channel or thread, use [`Guild.get_channel_or_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel_or_thread "discord.Guild.get_channel_or_thread")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   If you’re only looking to get threads, use [`Guild.get_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_thread "discord.Guild.get_thread")
    >      or [`TextChannel.get_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.get_thread "discord.TextChannel.get_thread")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `channel` parameter in [`on_guild_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update "discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   `channel` parameter in [`on_typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_typing "discord.on_typing")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Client.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_channel "discord.Client.fetch_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Client.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_channel "discord.Client.get_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channel "discord.Guild.fetch_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    

Removing In-Place Edits[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#removing-in-place-edits "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Most of the model methods that previously edited the model in-place have been updated to no longer do this. Instead, these methods will now return a new instance of the newly updated model. This has been done to avoid the library running into race conditions between in-place edits and gateway events on model updates. See [GH-4098](https://github.com/dolfies/discord.py-self/issues/4098)
 for more information.

Quick example:

content\_copy

\# before
await member.edit(nick\='new nick')
await member.send(f'Your new nick is {member.nick}')

\# after
updated\_member \= await member.edit(nick\='new nick')
await member.send(f'Your new nick is {updated\_member.nick}')

The following have been changed:

*   [`CategoryChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel.edit "discord.CategoryChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`CategoryChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel "discord.CategoryChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method only returns the updated [`Member`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member "discord.Member")
    >      when certain fields are updated.
    >     
    
*   [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`StageChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel "discord.StageChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`TextChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.edit "discord.TextChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`TextChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel "discord.TextChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`VoiceChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.edit "discord.VoiceChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`ClientUser.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ClientUser.edit "discord.ClientUser.edit")
    
*   [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
    
*   [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
    
*   [`Message.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.edit "discord.Message.edit")
    
*   [`Role.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Role.edit "discord.Role.edit")
    
*   [`Template.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Template.edit "discord.Template.edit")
    
*   [`Template.sync()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Template.sync "discord.Template.sync")
    
*   [`Webhook.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.edit "discord.Webhook.edit")
    
*   [`Webhook.edit_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.edit_message "discord.Webhook.edit_message")
    
*   [`WebhookMessage.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage.edit "discord.WebhookMessage.edit")
    

Sticker Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#sticker-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Discord has changed how their stickers work and as such, sticker support has been reworked.

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   Type of [`Message.stickers`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.stickers "discord.Message.stickers")
     changed to List\[[`StickerItem`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerItem "discord.StickerItem")\
    \].
    
    > *   To get the [`Sticker`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Sticker "discord.Sticker")
    >      from [`StickerItem`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerItem "discord.StickerItem")
    >     , use [`StickerItem.fetch()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerItem.fetch "discord.StickerItem.fetch")
    >      or (only for stickers from guilds the bot is in) [`Client.get_sticker()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_sticker "discord.Client.get_sticker")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Sticker.format`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Sticker.format "discord.Sticker.format")
     is now of [`StickerFormatType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerFormatType "discord.StickerFormatType")
     type.
    
*   `Sticker.tags` has been removed.
    
    > *   Depending on type of the sticker, [`StandardSticker.tags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StandardSticker.tags "discord.StandardSticker.tags")
    >      or [`GuildSticker.emoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.GuildSticker.emoji "discord.GuildSticker.emoji")
    >      can be used instead.
    >     
    
*   `Sticker.image` and related methods have been removed.
    
*   `Sticker.preview_image` and related methods have been removed.
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.type "discord.AuditLogDiff.type")
     is now of Union\[[`ChannelType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ChannelType "discord.ChannelType")\
    , [`StickerType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerType "discord.StickerType")\
    \] type.
    
*   The old `StickerType` enum has been renamed to [`StickerFormatType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerFormatType "discord.StickerFormatType")
    .
    
    > *   [`StickerType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StickerType "discord.StickerType")
    >      now refers to a sticker type (official sticker vs guild-uploaded sticker) rather than its format type.
    >     
    

Integrations Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#integrations-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To support the new integration types, integration support has been reworked.

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   The old `Integration` class has been renamed to [`StreamIntegration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StreamIntegration "discord.StreamIntegration")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.integrations()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.integrations "discord.Guild.integrations")
     now returns subclasses of the new [`Integration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Integration "discord.Integration")
     class.
    

Presence Updates Now Have A Separate Event[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#presence-updates-now-have-a-separate-event "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Presence updates (changes in member’s status and activity) now have a separate [`on_presence_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_presence_update "discord.on_presence_update")
 event. [`on_member_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_member_update "discord.on_member_update")
 event is now only called on member updates (changes in nickname, role, pending status, etc.).

From API perspective, these are separate events and as such, this change improves library’s consistency with the API. Presence updates usually are 90% of all handled events so splitting these should benefit listeners that were only interested in member updates.

Note that this event is also called when a [`Relationship`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Relationship "discord.Relationship")
 presence is updated.

Quick example:

content\_copy

\# before
@client.event
async def on\_member\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.nick != after.nick:
        await nick\_changed(before, after)
    if before.status != after.status:
        await status\_changed(before, after)

\# after
@client.event
async def on\_member\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.nick != after.nick:
        await nick\_changed(before, after)

@client.event
async def on\_presence\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.status != after.status:
        await status\_changed(before, after)

Moving Away From Custom AsyncIterator[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#moving-away-from-custom-asynciterator "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Asynchronous iterators in v1.0 were implemented using a special class named `AsyncIterator`. v2.0 instead provides regular asynchronous iterators with no added utility methods.

This means that usage of the following utility methods is no longer possible:

*   `AsyncIterator.next()`
    
    > Usage of an explicit `async for` loop should generally be preferred:
    > 
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > \# before
    > it \= channel.history()
    > while True:
    >     try:
    >         message \= await self.next()
    >     except discord.NoMoreItems:
    >         break
    >     print(f'Found message with ID {message.id}')
    > 
    > \# after
    > async for message in channel.history():
    >     print(f'Found message with ID {message.id}')
    > 
    > If you need to get next item from an iterator without a loop, you can use [`anext()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#anext "(in Python v3.11)")
    >  (new in Python 3.10) or [`__anext__()`](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__anext__ "(in Python v3.11)")
    >  instead:
    > 
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > \# before
    > it \= channel.history()
    > first \= await it.next()
    > if first.content \== 'do not iterate':
    >     return
    > async for message in it:
    >     ...
    > 
    > \# after
    > it \= channel.history()
    > first \= await anext(it)  \# await it.\_\_anext\_\_() on Python<3.10
    > if first.content \== 'do not iterate':
    >     return
    > async for message in it:
    >     ...
    
*   `AsyncIterator.get()`
    
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > \# before
    > msg \= await channel.history().get(author\_\_name\='Dave')
    > 
    > \# after
    > msg \= await discord.utils.get(channel.history(), author\_\_name\='Dave')
    
*   `AsyncIterator.find()`
    
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > def predicate(event):
    >     return event.reason is not None
    > 
    > \# before
    > event \= await guild.audit\_logs().find(predicate)
    > 
    > \# after
    > event \= await discord.utils.find(predicate, guild.audit\_logs())
    
*   `AsyncIterator.flatten()`
    
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > \# before
    > users \= await reaction.users().flatten()
    > 
    > \# after
    > users \= \[user async for user in reaction.users()\]
    
*   `AsyncIterator.chunk()`
    
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > \# before
    > async for leader, \*users in reaction.users().chunk(3):
    >     ...
    > 
    > \# after
    > async for leader, \*users in discord.utils.as\_chunks(reaction.users(), 3):
    >     ...
    
*   `AsyncIterator.map()`
    
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > \# before
    > content\_of\_messages \= \[\]
    > async for content in channel.history().map(lambda m: m.content):
    >     content\_of\_messages.append(content)
    > 
    > \# after
    > content\_of\_messages \= \[message.content async for message in channel.history()\]
    
*   `AsyncIterator.filter()`
    
    > content\_copy
    > 
    > def predicate(message):
    >     return not message.author.bot
    > 
    > \# before
    > user\_messages \= \[\]
    > async for message in channel.history().filter(lambda m: not m.author.bot):
    >     user\_messages.append(message)
    > 
    > \# after
    > user\_messages \= \[message async for message in channel.history() if not m.author.bot\]
    

To ease this transition, these changes have been made:

*   Added [`utils.as_chunks()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.as_chunks "discord.utils.as_chunks")
     as an alternative for `AsyncIter.chunk`.
    
*   Added support for [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.11)")
     to [`utils.find()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.find "discord.utils.find")
    .
    
*   Added support for [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.11)")
     to [`utils.get()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.get "discord.utils.get")
    .
    

The return type of the following methods has been changed to an [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.11)")
:

*   [`abc.Messageable.history()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.history "discord.abc.Messageable.history")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guilds()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guilds "discord.Client.fetch_guilds")
    
*   [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_members "discord.Guild.fetch_members")
    
*   [`Reaction.users()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Reaction.users "discord.Reaction.users")
    

The `NoMoreItems` exception was removed as calling [`anext()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#anext "(in Python v3.11)")
 or [`__anext__()`](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__anext__ "(in Python v3.11)")
 on an [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.11)")
 will now raise [`StopAsyncIteration`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#StopAsyncIteration "(in Python v3.11)")
.

Changing certain lists to be lazy sequences instead[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#changing-certain-lists-to-be-lazy-sequences-instead "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to improve performance when calculating the length of certain lists, certain attributes were changed to return a sequence rather than a [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.11)")
.

A sequence is similar to a [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.11)")
 except it is read-only. In order to get a list again you can call [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.11)")
 on the resulting sequence.

The following properties were changed to return a sequence instead of a list:

*   [`Client.guilds`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.guilds "discord.Client.guilds")
    
*   [`Client.emojis`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.emojis "discord.Client.emojis")
    
*   [`Client.private_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.private_channels "discord.Client.private_channels")
    
*   [`Guild.roles`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.roles "discord.Guild.roles")
    
*   [`Guild.channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.channels "discord.Guild.channels")
    
*   [`Guild.members`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.members "discord.Guild.members")
    

This change should be transparent, unless you are modifying the sequence by doing things such as `list.append`.

Embed Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#embed-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Originally, embeds used a special sentinel to denote emptiness or remove an attribute from display. The `Embed.Empty` sentinel was made when Discord’s embed design was in a nebulous state of flux. Since then, the embed design has stabilised and thus the sentinel is seen as legacy.

Therefore, `Embed.Empty` has been removed in favour of `None`.

Additionally, `Embed.__eq__` has been implemented thus embeds becoming unhashable (e.g. using them in sets or dict keys).

content\_copy

\# before
embed \= discord.Embed(title\='foo')
embed.title \= discord.Embed.Empty
embed \== embed.copy() \# False

\# after
embed \= discord.Embed(title\='foo')
embed.title \= None
embed \== embed.copy() \# True
{embed, embed} \# Raises TypeError

Removal of `InvalidArgument` Exception[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#removal-of-invalidargument-exception "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The custom `InvalidArgument` exception has been removed and functions and methods that raised it are now raising [`TypeError`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.11)")
 and/or [`ValueError`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.11)")
 instead.

The following methods have been changed:

*   [`Message.add_reaction()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.add_reaction "discord.Message.add_reaction")
    
*   `AutoShardedClient.change_presence()`
    
*   [`Client.change_presence()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.change_presence "discord.Client.change_presence")
