Logging Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#logging-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The library now provides a default logging configuration if using [`Client.run()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.run "discord.Client.run")
. To disable it, pass `None` to the `log_handler` keyword parameter. Since the library now provides a default logging configuration, certain methods were changed to no longer print to [`sys.stderr`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.stderr "(in Python v3.11)")
 but use the logger instead:

*   [`Client.on_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.on_error "discord.Client.on_error")
    
*   [`discord.ext.tasks.Loop.error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.error "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.error")
    
*   [`discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error "discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error")
    
*   [`VoiceClient.play()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceClient.play "discord.VoiceClient.play")
    

For more information, check [Setting Up Logging](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/logging.html)
.

Text in Voice[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#text-in-voice "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to support text in voice functionality, a few changes had to be made:

*   [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
     is now [`abc.Messageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable "discord.abc.Messageable")
     so it can have messages sent and received.
    
*   [`Message.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.channel "discord.Message.channel")
     can now be [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
     and [`StageChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel "discord.StageChannel")
    .
    

Removal of `StoreChannel`[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#removal-of-storechannel "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Discord’s API has removed store channels as of [March 10th, 2022](https://support-dev.discord.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309018858647)
. Therefore, the library has removed support for it as well.

This removes the following:

*   `StoreChannel`
    
*   `commands.StoreChannelConverter`
    

Change in `Guild.bans` endpoint[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#change-in-guild-bans-endpoint "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Due to a breaking API change by Discord, [`Guild.bans()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.bans "discord.Guild.bans")
 no longer returns a list of every ban in the guild but instead is paginated using an asynchronous iterator.

content\_copy

\# before

bans \= await guild.bans()

\# after
async for ban in guild.bans(limit\=1000):
    ...

Function Signature Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#function-signature-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Parameters in the following methods are now all positional-only:

*   [`Client.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_channel "discord.Client.get_channel")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_channel "discord.Client.fetch_channel")
    
*   [`Guild.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel "discord.Guild.get_channel")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channel "discord.Guild.fetch_channel")
    
*   [`Client.get_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_emoji "discord.Client.get_emoji")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_emoji "discord.Guild.fetch_emoji")
    
*   [`Client.get_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_guild "discord.Client.get_guild")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guild "discord.Client.fetch_guild")
    
*   [`Client.delete_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.delete_invite "discord.Client.delete_invite")
    
*   [`Guild.get_member()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_member "discord.Guild.get_member")
    
*   [`Guild.get_member_named()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_member_named "discord.Guild.get_member_named")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_member()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_member "discord.Guild.fetch_member")
    
*   [`Client.get_user()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_user "discord.Client.get_user")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_user()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_user "discord.Client.fetch_user")
    
*   [`Guild.get_role()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_role "discord.Guild.get_role")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_webhook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_webhook "discord.Client.fetch_webhook")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_widget()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_widget "discord.Client.fetch_widget")
    
*   [`Message.add_reaction()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.add_reaction "discord.Message.add_reaction")
    
*   [`Client.on_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.on_error "discord.Client.on_error")
    
*   [`abc.Messageable.fetch_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.fetch_message "discord.abc.Messageable.fetch_message")
    
*   [`abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for "discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for")
    
*   [`DMChannel.get_partial_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.DMChannel.get_partial_message "discord.DMChannel.get_partial_message")
    
*   [`TextChannel.get_partial_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.get_partial_message "discord.TextChannel.get_partial_message")
    
*   [`TextChannel.delete_messages()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.delete_messages "discord.TextChannel.delete_messages")
    
*   [`Webhook.delete_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.delete_message "discord.Webhook.delete_message")
    
*   [`utils.find()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.find "discord.utils.find")
    
*   [`utils.snowflake_time()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.snowflake_time "discord.utils.snowflake_time")
    

The following parameters are now positional-only:

*   `iterable` in [`utils.get()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.get "discord.utils.get")
    
*   `event_method` in [`Client.on_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.on_error "discord.Client.on_error")
    
*   `event` in [`Client.wait_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.wait_for "discord.Client.wait_for")
    
*   `dt` in [`utils.time_snowflake()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.time_snowflake "discord.utils.time_snowflake")
    

The following are now keyword-only:

*   Parameters in [`Reaction.users()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Reaction.users "discord.Reaction.users")
    
*   Parameters in [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   `permissions`, `guild`, `redirect_uri`, and `scopes` parameters in [`utils.oauth_url()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.oauth_url "discord.utils.oauth_url")
    
*   `high` in [`utils.snowflake_time()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.snowflake_time "discord.utils.snowflake_time")
    

The library now less often uses `None` as the default value for function/method parameters.

As a result, these parameters can no longer be `None`:

*   `size`, `format`, and `static_format` in [`Asset.replace()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    
*   `check` in [`TextChannel.purge()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.purge "discord.TextChannel.purge")
    
*   `icon` and `code` in [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   `roles` in [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
    
*   `topic`, `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_text_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_text_channel "discord.Guild.create_text_channel")
    
*   `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_voice_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_voice_channel "discord.Guild.create_voice_channel")
    
*   `topic`, `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_stage_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_stage_channel "discord.Guild.create_stage_channel")
    
*   `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_category()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_category "discord.Guild.create_category")
    
*   `roles` in [`Guild.prune_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.prune_members "discord.Guild.prune_members")
    
*   `roles` in [`Guild.estimate_pruned_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.estimate_pruned_members "discord.Guild.estimate_pruned_members")
    
*   `description` in [`Guild.create_template()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_template "discord.Guild.create_template")
    
*   `roles` in [`Guild.create_custom_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji "discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji")
    
*   `before`, `after`, `oldest_first`, `user`, and `action` in [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
    
*   `enable_emoticons` in [`StreamIntegration.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StreamIntegration.edit "discord.StreamIntegration.edit")
    
*   `mute`, `deafen`, `suppress`, and `roles` in [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
*   `position` in [`Role.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Role.edit "discord.Role.edit")
    
*   `icon` in [`Template.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Template.create_guild "discord.Template.create_guild")
    
*   `name` in [`Template.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Template.edit "discord.Template.edit")
    
*   `permissions`, `guild`, `redirect_uri`, `scopes` in [`utils.oauth_url()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.oauth_url "discord.utils.oauth_url")
    
*   `content`, `username`, `avatar_url`, `tts`, `file`, `files`, `embed`, `embeds`, and `allowed_mentions` in [`Webhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.send "discord.Webhook.send")
    

Allowed types for the following parameters have been changed:

*   `rtc_region` in [`Guild.create_voice_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_voice_channel "discord.Guild.create_voice_channel")
     is now of type Optional\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")\
    \].
    
*   `rtc_region` in [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
     is now of type Optional\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")\
    \].
    
*   `rtc_region` in [`VoiceChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.edit "discord.VoiceChannel.edit")
     is now of type Optional\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")\
    \].
    
*   `preferred_locale` in [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
     is now of type [`Locale`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Locale "discord.Locale")
    .
    

Attribute Type Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#attribute-type-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following changes have been made:

*   [`DMChannel.recipient`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.DMChannel.recipient "discord.DMChannel.recipient")
     may now be `None`.
    
*   [`Guild.vanity_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.vanity_invite "discord.Guild.vanity_invite")
     may now be `None`. This has been done to fix an issue with the method returning a broken [`Invite`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Invite "discord.Invite")
     object.
    
*   [`Widget.fetch_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Widget.fetch_invite "discord.Widget.fetch_invite")
     may now be `None`.
    
*   [`Guild.mfa_level`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.mfa_level "discord.Guild.mfa_level")
     is now of type [`MFALevel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.MFALevel "discord.MFALevel")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.member_count`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.member_count "discord.Guild.member_count")
     is now of type Optional\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.11)")\
    \].
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.mfa_level`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.mfa_level "discord.AuditLogDiff.mfa_level")
     is now of type [`MFALevel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.MFALevel "discord.MFALevel")
    .
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.rtc_region`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.rtc_region "discord.AuditLogDiff.rtc_region")
     is now of type [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
    .
    
*   [`StageChannel.rtc_region`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.rtc_region "discord.StageChannel.rtc_region")
     is now of type [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
    .
    
*   [`VoiceChannel.rtc_region`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.rtc_region "discord.VoiceChannel.rtc_region")
     is now of type [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
    .
    
*   [`ClientUser.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ClientUser.avatar "discord.ClientUser.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a user has displayed, consider [`ClientUser.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ClientUser.display_avatar "discord.ClientUser.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Member.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member.avatar "discord.Member.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a member or user has displayed, consider [`Member.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Member.display_avatar "discord.Member.display_avatar")
    >      or [`User.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.User.display_avatar "discord.User.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`User.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.User.avatar "discord.User.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a user has displayed, consider [`User.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.User.display_avatar "discord.User.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Webhook.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.avatar "discord.Webhook.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a webhook has displayed, consider [`Webhook.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.display_avatar "discord.Webhook.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`AuditLogEntry.target`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.target "discord.AuditLogEntry.target")
     may now be a [`PartialMessageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.PartialMessageable "discord.PartialMessageable")
    .
    
*   [`PartialMessage.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.PartialMessage.channel "discord.PartialMessage.channel")
     may now be a [`PartialMessageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.PartialMessageable "discord.PartialMessageable")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.preferred_locale`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.preferred_locale "discord.Guild.preferred_locale")
     is now of type [`Locale`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Locale "discord.Locale")
    .
    
*   [`abc.GuildChannel.overwrites`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.overwrites "discord.abc.GuildChannel.overwrites")
     keys can now have [`Object`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Object "discord.Object")
     in them.
    

Removals[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#removals "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following deprecated functionality have been removed:

*   `Client.request_offline_members`
    
    > *   Use [`Guild.chunk()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.chunk "discord.Guild.chunk")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `AutoShardedClient.request_offline_members`
    
    > *   Use [`Guild.chunk()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.chunk "discord.Guild.chunk")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `Client.logout`
    
    > *   Use [`Client.close()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.close "discord.Client.close")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `fetch_offline_members` parameter from [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
     constructor
    
    > *   Use `chunk_guild_at_startup` instead.
    >     
    
*   `Permissions.use_slash_commands` and `PermissionOverwrite.use_slash_commands`
    
    *   Use [`Permissions.use_application_commands`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.use_application_commands "discord.Permissions.use_application_commands")
         and `PermissionOverwrite.use_application_commands` instead.
        
    

The following have been removed:

*   `MemberCacheFlags.online`
    
    > *   There is no replacement for this one. The current API version no longer provides enough data for this to be possible.
    >     
    
*   `User.permissions_in` and `Member.permissions_in`
    
    > *   Use [`abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for "discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `guild_subscriptions` parameter from [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
     constructor
    
    > *   The current API version no longer provides this functionality.
    >     
    
*   `guild_subscription_options` parameter from [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
     constructor
    
    > *   The auto-subscribing system has been removed. `chunk_guilds_at_startup` is now utilised properly.
    >     
    
*   [`VerificationLevel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel "discord.VerificationLevel")
     aliases:
    
    > *   `VerificationLevel.table_flip` - use [`VerificationLevel.high`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.high "discord.VerificationLevel.high")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   `VerificationLevel.extreme` - use [`VerificationLevel.highest`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.highest "discord.VerificationLevel.highest")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   `VerificationLevel.double_table_flip` - use [`VerificationLevel.highest`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.highest "discord.VerificationLevel.highest")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   `VerificationLevel.very_high` - use [`VerificationLevel.highest`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.highest "discord.VerificationLevel.highest")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `topic` parameter from [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
    
    > *   The `topic` parameter must now be set via [`StageChannel.create_instance()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.create_instance "discord.StageChannel.create_instance")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   `Reaction.custom_emoji`
    
    > *   Use [`Reaction.is_custom_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Reaction.is_custom_emoji "discord.Reaction.is_custom_emoji")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `AuditLogDiff.region`
    
*   `Guild.region`
    
*   `VoiceRegion`
    
    > *   This has been marked deprecated by Discord and it was usually more or less out of date due to the pace they added them anyway.
    >     
    
*   `region` parameter from [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   `region` parameter from [`Template.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Template.create_guild "discord.Template.create_guild")
    
*   `region` parameter from [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
    
*   The undocumented private `on_socket_response` event
    
    > *   Consider using the newer documented [`on_socket_event_type()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_socket_event_type "discord.on_socket_event_type")
    >      event instead.
    >     
    
*   `abc.Messageable.trigger_typing`
    
    > *   Use [`abc.Messageable.typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.typing "discord.abc.Messageable.typing")
    >      with `await` instead.
    >     
    

Miscellaneous Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#miscellaneous-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following changes have been made:

*   [`on_socket_raw_receive()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_socket_raw_receive "discord.on_socket_raw_receive")
     is now only called if `enable_debug_events` is set on [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
    .
    
*   [`on_socket_raw_receive()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_socket_raw_receive "discord.on_socket_raw_receive")
     is now only called once the **complete** message is received and decompressed. The passed `msg` parameter is now always [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.11)")
    .
    
*   [`on_socket_raw_send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.on_socket_raw_send "discord.on_socket_raw_send")
     is now only called if `enable_debug_events` is set on [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
    .
    
*   The documented return type for [`Guild.fetch_channels()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channels "discord.Guild.fetch_channels")
     changed to Sequence\[[`abc.GuildChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel "discord.abc.GuildChannel")\
    \].
    
*   [`utils.resolve_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.resolve_invite "discord.utils.resolve_invite")
     now returns a [`ResolvedInvite`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ResolvedInvite "discord.ResolvedInvite")
     class.
    
*   [`utils.oauth_url()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.utils.oauth_url "discord.utils.oauth_url")
     now defaults to `bot` and `applications.commands` scopes when not given instead of just `bot`.
    
*   [`abc.Messageable.typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.typing "discord.abc.Messageable.typing")
     can no longer be used as a regular (non-async) context manager.
    
*   `created_at` is no longer part of [`abc.Snowflake`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.abc.Snowflake "discord.abc.Snowflake")
    .
    
    > All of the existing classes still keep this attribute. It is just no longer part of this protocol. This has been done because Discord reuses IDs (snowflakes) of some models in other models. For example, if [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >  is created from a message, its [`Thread.id`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.id "discord.Thread.id")
    >  is equivalent to the ID of that message and as such it doesn’t contain information about creation time of the thread and [`Thread.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread.created_at "discord.Thread.created_at")
    >  cannot be based on it.
    
*   [`Embed`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Embed "discord.Embed")
    ’s bool implementation now returns `True` when embed has any data set.
    
*   Calling [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
     without `roles` argument no longer makes the emoji available to everyone.
    
    > *   To make the emoji available to everyone, pass an empty list to `roles` instead.
    >     
    
*   The old `Colour.blurple` has been renamed to [`Colour.og_blurple`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Colour.og_blurple "discord.Colour.og_blurple")
    .
    
    > *   [`Colour.blurple`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Colour.blurple "discord.Colour.blurple")
    >      refers to a different colour now.
    >     
    
*   [`Message.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.type "discord.Message.type")
     is now set to [`MessageType.reply`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.MessageType.reply "discord.MessageType.reply")
     when a message is a reply.
    
    > *   This is caused by a difference in behavior in the current Discord API version.
    >     
    
*   [`Message.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message.edit "discord.Message.edit")
     now merges object passed in `allowed_mentions` parameter with [`Client.allowed_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.allowed_mentions "discord.Client.allowed_mentions")
    . If the parameter isn’t provided, the defaults given by [`Client.allowed_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Client.allowed_mentions "discord.Client.allowed_mentions")
     are used instead.
    
*   [`Permissions.stage_moderator()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.stage_moderator "discord.Permissions.stage_moderator")
     now includes the [`Permissions.manage_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_channels "discord.Permissions.manage_channels")
     permission and the [`Permissions.request_to_speak`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.request_to_speak "discord.Permissions.request_to_speak")
     permission is no longer included.
    
*   [`File.filename`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.File.filename "discord.File.filename")
     will no longer be `None`, in situations where previously this was the case the filename is set to `'untitled'`.
    

[`VoiceProtocol.connect()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceProtocol.connect "discord.VoiceProtocol.connect")
 signature changes.[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#voiceprotocol-connect-signature-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`VoiceProtocol.connect()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.VoiceProtocol.connect "discord.VoiceProtocol.connect")
 will now be passed 2 keyword only arguments, `self_deaf` and `self_mute`. These indicate whether or not the client should join the voice chat being deafened or muted.

Command Extension Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#command-extension-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#extension-and-cog-loading-unloading-is-now-asynchronous "Permalink to this headline")

As an extension to the [asyncio changes](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-client-async-setup)
 the loading and unloading of extensions and cogs is now asynchronous.

To accommodate this, the following changes have been made:

*   The `setup` and `teardown` functions in extensions must now be coroutines.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.load_extension()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.load_extension "discord.ext.commands.Bot.load_extension")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.unload_extension()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.unload_extension "discord.ext.commands.Bot.unload_extension")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.reload_extension()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.reload_extension "discord.ext.commands.Bot.reload_extension")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.add_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog")
     must now be awaited.
    

Quick example of an extension setup function:

content\_copy

\# before
def setup(bot):
    bot.add\_cog(MyCog(bot))

\# after
async def setup(bot):
    await bot.add\_cog(MyCog(bot))

Quick example of loading an extension:

content\_copy

\# before
bot.load\_extension('my\_extension')

\# after using setup\_hook
class MyBot(commands.Bot):
    async def setup\_hook(self):
        await self.load\_extension('my\_extension')

\# after using async\_with
async def main():
    async with bot:
        await bot.load\_extension('my\_extension')
        await bot.start(TOKEN)

asyncio.run(main())

### Converters Are Now Generic Runtime Protocols[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#converters-are-now-generic-runtime-protocols "Permalink to this headline")

[`Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")
 is now a [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.11)")
 [`typing.Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.11)")
.

This results in a change of the base metaclass used by these classes which may affect user-created classes that inherit from [`Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")
.

Quick example:

content\_copy

\# before
class SomeConverterMeta(type):
    ...

class SomeConverter(commands.Converter, metaclass\=SomeConverterMeta):
    ...

\# after
class SomeConverterMeta(type(commands.Converter)):
    ...

class SomeConverter(commands.Converter, metaclass\=SomeConverterMeta):
    ...

In addition, [`Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")
 is now a [`typing.Generic`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generic "(in Python v3.11)")
 which (optionally) allows the users to define their type hints more accurately.

### Function Signature Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#id1 "Permalink to this headline")

Parameters in the following methods are now all positional-only:

*   [`ext.commands.when_mentioned()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.when_mentioned "discord.ext.commands.when_mentioned")

The following parameters are now positional-only:

*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.check_once()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.check_once "discord.ext.commands.Bot.check_once")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener")
    
*   `message` in [`ext.commands.Bot.get_context()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_context "discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_context")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Command.add_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.add_check "discord.ext.commands.Command.add_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Command.remove_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.remove_check "discord.ext.commands.Command.remove_check")
    
*   `context` in [`ext.commands.Command.__call__()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.__call__ "discord.ext.commands.Command.__call__")
    
*   `commands` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.filter_commands()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.filter_commands "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.filter_commands")
    
*   `ctx` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.command_callback()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.command_callback "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.command_callback")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.add_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.add_check "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.add_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.remove_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.remove_check "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.remove_check")
    
*   `commands` in [`ext.commands.DefaultHelpCommand.add_indented_commands()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.DefaultHelpCommand.add_indented_commands "discord.ext.commands.DefaultHelpCommand.add_indented_commands")
    
*   `cog` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.get_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_cog")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog")
    
*   `command` in [`ext.commands.Context.invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.invoke "discord.ext.commands.Context.invoke")
    
*   `command` in [`ext.commands.GroupMixin.add_command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.add_command "discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.add_command")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.GroupMixin.get_command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.get_command "discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.get_command")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.GroupMixin.remove_command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.remove_command "discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.remove_command")
    

The following parameters have been added:

*   `user_bot` in [`Bot`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot "discord.ext.commands.Bot")
    
    > *   Allows your bot to reply to everyone’s message.
    >     
    

The library now less often uses `None` as the default value for function/method parameters.

As a result, these parameters can no longer be `None`:

*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.listen()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.listen "discord.ext.commands.Bot.listen")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Cog.listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cog.listener "discord.ext.commands.Cog.listener")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command "discord.ext.commands.Command")
    
*   `name` and `cls` in [`ext.commands.command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.command "discord.ext.commands.command")
    
*   `name` and `cls` in [`ext.commands.group()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.group "discord.ext.commands.group")
    

### Removals[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#id2 "Permalink to this headline")

The following attributes have been removed:

*   `original` from the [`ExtensionNotFound`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound")
    
*   `type` from the [`Cooldown`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cooldown "discord.ext.commands.Cooldown")
     class that was provided by the [`ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.cooldown`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.cooldown "discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.cooldown")
     attribute
    
    > *   Use [`ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.type "discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.type")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `clean_prefix` from the [`HelpCommand`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand")
    
    > *   Use [`ext.commands.Context.clean_prefix`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.clean_prefix "discord.ext.commands.Context.clean_prefix")
    >      instead.
    >     
    

### Miscellaneous Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#id3 "Permalink to this headline")

*   [`ext.commands.Bot.add_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog")
     is now raising [`ClientException`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.ClientException "discord.ClientException")
     when a cog with the same name is already loaded.
    
    > *   To override a cog, the new `override` parameter can be used.
    >     
    
*   When passing a callable to `type` argument of [`cooldown()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.cooldown "discord.ext.commands.cooldown")
    , it now needs to accept [`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
     rather than [`Message`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Message "discord.Message")
     as its only argument.
    
*   Metaclass of [`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
     changed from [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.11)")
     to [`type`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.11)")
    .
    
*   Changed type of [`ext.commands.Command.clean_params`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params "discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params")
     from [`collections.OrderedDict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.html#collections.OrderedDict "(in Python v3.11)")
     to [`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.11)")
    . as the latter is guaranteed to preserve insertion order since Python 3.7.
    
*   [`ext.commands.ChannelNotReadable.argument`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ChannelNotReadable.argument "discord.ext.commands.ChannelNotReadable.argument")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
     due to the [Thread Support](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-thread-support)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.NSFWChannelRequired.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.NSFWChannelRequired.channel "discord.ext.commands.NSFWChannelRequired.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
     due to the [Thread Support](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-thread-support)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Context.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.channel "discord.ext.commands.Context.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
     due to the [Thread Support](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-thread-support)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Context.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.channel "discord.ext.commands.Context.channel")
     may now be a [`PartialMessageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/api.html#discord.PartialMessageable "discord.PartialMessageable")
    .
    
*   `MissingPermissions.missing_perms` has been renamed to [`ext.commands.MissingPermissions.missing_permissions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.MissingPermissions.missing_permissions "discord.ext.commands.MissingPermissions.missing_permissions")
    .
    
*   `BotMissingPermissions.missing_perms` has been renamed to [`ext.commands.BotMissingPermissions.missing_permissions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.BotMissingPermissions.missing_permissions "discord.ext.commands.BotMissingPermissions.missing_permissions")
    .
    
*   [`ext.commands.Cog.cog_load()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_load "discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_load")
     has been added as part of the [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Cog.cog_unload()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_unload "discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_unload")
     may now be a [coroutine](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-coroutine "(in Python v3.11)")
     due to the [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.clean_params`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params "discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params")
     type now uses a custom [`inspect.Parameter`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/inspect.html#inspect.Parameter "(in Python v3.11)")
     to handle defaults.
    

Tasks Extension Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/migrating.html#tasks-extension-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Calling [`ext.tasks.Loop.stop()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.stop "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.stop")
     in [`before_loop()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.before_loop "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.before_loop")
     now stops the first iteration from running.
    
*   Calling [`ext.tasks.Loop.change_interval()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.change_interval "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.change_interval")
     now changes the interval for the sleep time right away, rather than on the next loop iteration.
    
*   `loop` parameter in [`ext.tasks.loop()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.loop "discord.ext.tasks.loop")
     can no longer be `None`.
    

arrow\_upward to top

# Migrating to v2.0

View Documentation For discord discord.ext.commands discord.ext.tasks

 search

settings

Migrating to v2.0[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-to-v2-0 "Permalink to this headline")

====================================================================================================================================

Compared to v1.0, v2.0 mostly has breaking changes related to better developer experience and API coverage. While the changes aren’t as massive to require an entire rewrite, there are still many changes that need to be accounted for.

This is the first version to have version guarantees apply.

Below is a **non-exhaustive** list of changes.

Python Version Change[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#python-version-change "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to ease development, maintain security updates, and use newer features **v2.0 drops support for Python 3.7 and earlier**.

asyncio Event Loop Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#asyncio-event-loop-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Python 3.7 introduced a new helper function [`asyncio.run()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-runner.html#asyncio.run "(in Python v3.14)")
 which automatically creates and destroys the asynchronous event loop.

In order to support this, the way discord.py-self handles the [`asyncio`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html#module-asyncio "(in Python v3.14)")
 event loop has changed.

This allows you to rather than using [`Client.run()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.run "discord.Client.run")
 create your own asynchronous loop to setup other asynchronous code as needed.

Quick example:

client \= discord.Client()

async def main():
    \# do other async things
    await my\_async\_function()

    \# start the client
    async with client:
        await client.start(TOKEN)

asyncio.run(main())

A new [`setup_hook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.setup_hook "discord.Client.setup_hook")
 method has also been added to the [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 class. This method is called after login but before connecting to the discord gateway.

It is intended to be used to setup various bot features in an asynchronous context.

[`setup_hook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.setup_hook "discord.Client.setup_hook")
 can be defined by subclassing the [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 class.

Quick example:

class MyClient(discord.Client):
    async def setup\_hook(self):
        print('This is asynchronous!')

client \= MyClient()
client.run(TOKEN)

With this change, constructor of [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 no longer accepts `connector` and `loop` parameters.

In parallel with this change, changes were made to loading and unloading of commands extension extensions and cogs, see [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
 for more information.

Abstract Base Classes Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#abstract-base-classes-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Abstract Base Classes](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord-api-abcs)
 that inherited from [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.14)")
 now inherit from [`typing.Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
.

This results in a change of the base metaclass used by these classes but this should generally be completely transparent to the user.

All of the classes are either [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.14)")
 protocols or explicitly inherited from and as such usage with [`isinstance()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#isinstance "(in Python v3.14)")
 and [`issubclass()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#issubclass "(in Python v3.14)")
 is not affected.

The following have been changed to [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.14)")
 [`Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
s:

*   [`abc.Snowflake`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Snowflake "discord.abc.Snowflake")
    
*   [`abc.User`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.User "discord.abc.User")
    

The following have been changed to subclass [`Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
:

*   [`abc.GuildChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel "discord.abc.GuildChannel")
    
*   [`abc.Connectable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Connectable "discord.abc.Connectable")
    

The following have been changed to use the default metaclass instead of [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.14)")
:

*   [`abc.Messageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable "discord.abc.Messageable")
    
*   [`abc.PrivateChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.PrivateChannel "discord.abc.PrivateChannel")
    

`datetime` Objects Are Now UTC-Aware[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#datetime-objects-are-now-utc-aware "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All usage of naive [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects in the library has been replaced with aware objects using UTC timezone. Methods that accepted naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects now also accept timezone-aware objects. To keep behavior inline with [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
’s methods, this library’s methods now assume that naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects are local time (note that some of the methods may not accept naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
, such exceptions are listed below).

Because naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects are treated by many of its methods as local times, the previous behavior was more likely to result in programming errors with their usage.

To ease the migration [`utils.utcnow()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.utcnow "discord.utils.utcnow")
 helper function has been added.

Warning

Using [`datetime.datetime.utcnow()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.utcnow "(in Python v3.14)")
 can be problematic since it returns a naive UTC `datetime` object.

Quick example:

\# before
week\_ago \= datetime.datetime.utcnow() \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
if member.created\_at \> week\_ago:
    print(f'Member account {member} was created less than a week ago!')

\# after
\# The new helper function can be used here:
week\_ago \= discord.utils.utcnow() \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
\# ...or the equivalent result can be achieved with datetime.datetime.now():
week\_ago \= datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc) \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
if member.created\_at \> week\_ago:
    print(f'Member account {member} was created less than a week ago!')

The following have been changed from naive to aware [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects in UTC:

*   [`AuditLogEntry.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.created_at "discord.AuditLogEntry.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`BaseActivity.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.BaseActivity.created_at "discord.BaseActivity.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`ClientUser.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ClientUser.created_at "discord.ClientUser.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`DMChannel.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.DMChannel.created_at "discord.DMChannel.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Emoji.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.created_at "discord.Emoji.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`GroupChannel.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.GroupChannel.created_at "discord.GroupChannel.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Guild.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.created_at "discord.Guild.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`abc.GuildChannel.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.created_at "discord.abc.GuildChannel.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Invite.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Invite.created_at "discord.Invite.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Object.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Object.created_at "discord.Object.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Member.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member.created_at "discord.Member.created_at")
     attribute

     attribute

     attribute

     attribute
    
*   [`Embed.timestamp`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Embed.timestamp "discord.Embed.timestamp")
     attribute

     attribute
    
*   `timestamp` parameter in [`on_typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_typing "discord.on_typing")
     event
    
*   `last_pin` parameter in [`on_private_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_private_channel_pins_update "discord.on_private_channel_pins_update")
     event
    
*   `last_pin` parameter in [`on_guild_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update "discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update")
     event
    
*   Return type of [`utils.snowflake_time()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.snowflake_time "discord.utils.snowflake_time")
    

The following now accept aware [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 and assume that if the passed [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 is naive, it is a local time:

*   [`abc.Messageable.history()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.history "discord.abc.Messageable.history")
     method
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guilds()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guilds "discord.Client.fetch_guilds")
     method
    
*   [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
     method
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_members "discord.Guild.fetch_members")
     method
    
*   [`TextChannel.purge()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel.purge "discord.TextChannel.purge")
     method
    
*   [`Embed`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Embed "discord.Embed")
     constructor
    
*   [`Embed.timestamp`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Embed.timestamp "discord.Embed.timestamp")
     property setter
    
*   [`utils.sleep_until()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.sleep_until "discord.utils.sleep_until")
     function
    
*   [`utils.time_snowflake()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.time_snowflake "discord.utils.time_snowflake")
     function
    

Currently, there’s only one place in this library that doesn’t accept naive [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects:

*   `timed_out_until` parameter in [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
    > This has been done to prevent users from mistakenly applying incorrect timeouts to guild members.
    

Major Webhook Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#major-webhook-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Webhook support has been rewritten to work better with typings and rate limits.

As a result, synchronous functionality has been split to separate classes.

Quick example for asynchronous webhooks:

\# before
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    webhook \= discord.Webhook.from\_url('url-here', adapter\=discord.AsyncWebhookAdapter(session))
    await webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

\# after
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    webhook \= discord.Webhook.from\_url('url-here', session\=session)
    await webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

Quick example for synchronous webhooks:

\# before
webhook \= discord.Webhook.partial(123456, 'token-here', adapter\=discord.RequestsWebhookAdapter())
webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

\# after
webhook \= discord.SyncWebhook.partial(123456, 'token-here')
webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   Synchronous functionality of [`Webhook`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook "discord.Webhook")
     and [`WebhookMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage "discord.WebhookMessage")
     has been split to [`SyncWebhook`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.SyncWebhook "discord.SyncWebhook")
     and [`SyncWebhookMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.SyncWebhookMessage "discord.SyncWebhookMessage")
    .
    
*   `WebhookAdapter` class has been removed and the interfaces based on it (`AsyncWebhookAdapter` and `RequestsWebhookAdapter`) are now considered implementation detail and should not be depended on.
    
*   `execute` alias for [`Webhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.send "discord.Webhook.send")
    /[`SyncWebhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.SyncWebhook.send "discord.SyncWebhook.send")
     has been removed.
    

Asset Redesign and Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#asset-redesign-and-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 object now encompasses all of the methods and attributes related to a CDN asset.

This means that all models with asset-related attribute and methods have been transformed to use this new design. As an example, here’s how these changes look for [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
 (of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 type):

*   `Guild.icon` (of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
     type) has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    .
    
*   `Guild.is_icon_animated` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.is_animated`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.is_animated "discord.Asset.is_animated")
    .
    
*   `Guild.icon_url` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
    .
    
*   `Guild.icon_url_as` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    .
    
    > *   Helper methods [`Asset.with_size()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.with_size "discord.Asset.with_size")
    >     , [`Asset.with_format()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.with_format "discord.Asset.with_format")
    >     , and [`Asset.with_static_format()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.with_static_format "discord.Asset.with_static_format")
    >      have also been added.
    >     
    

In addition to this, [`Emoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji "discord.Emoji")
 and [`PartialEmoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PartialEmoji "discord.PartialEmoji")
 now also share an interface similar to [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
’s:

*   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
     is now of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
     type.
    
*   `Emoji.url_as` has been removed.
    
*   `Emoji.url.read` has been replaced with [`Emoji.read()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.read "discord.Emoji.read")
    .
    
*   `Emoji.url.save` has been replaced with [`Emoji.save()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.save "discord.Emoji.save")
    .
    

[`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 now always represent an actually existing CDN asset. This means that:

*   `str(x)` on an [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can no longer return an empty string.
    
*   `bool(x)` on an [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can no longer return `False`.
    
*   Attributes containing an optional [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can now be `None`.
    

The following were affected by this change:

*   [`AuditLogDiff`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff "discord.AuditLogDiff")
    
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.avatar "discord.AuditLogDiff.avatar")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.icon "discord.AuditLogDiff.icon")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.splash "discord.AuditLogDiff.splash")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >

    > *   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
    >      is now of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    >      type.
    >

    >

    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url.save` (replaced by [`Emoji.save()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.save "discord.Emoji.save")
    >     )
