Thread Support[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#thread-support "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

v2.0 has been updated to use a newer API gateway version which supports threads and as a result of this had to make few breaking changes. Most notably messages sent in guilds can, in addition to a [`TextChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel "discord.TextChannel")
, be sent in a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
.

The main differences between text channels and threads are:

*   Threads do not have their own permissions, they inherit the permissions of their parent channel.
    
    > *   This means that threads do not have these attributes:
    >     
    >     > *   `changed_roles`
    >     >     
    >     > *   `overwrites`
    >     >     
    >     > *   `permissions_synced`
    >     >     
    >     
    > 
    > Note
    > 
    > Text channels have a few dedicated permissions for threads:
    > 
    > *   [`Permissions.manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.create_public_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.create_public_threads "discord.Permissions.create_public_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.create_private_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.create_private_threads "discord.Permissions.create_private_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own NSFW status, they inherit it from their parent channel.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have an `nsfw` attribute.
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own topic.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have a `topic` attribute.
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own position in the channel list.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have a `position` attribute.
    >     
    
*   [`Thread.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.created_at "discord.Thread.created_at")
     of threads created before 10 January 2022 is `None`.
    
*   [`Thread.members`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.members "discord.Thread.members")
     is of type List\[[`ThreadMember`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ThreadMember "discord.ThreadMember")\
    \] rather than List\[[`Member`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member "discord.Member")\
    \]
    
    > *   Most of the time, this data is not provided and a call to [`Thread.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.fetch_members "discord.Thread.fetch_members")
    >      to fetch the partial objects is needed.
    >     
    

For convenience, [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
 has a set of properties and methods that return the information about the parent channel:

*   [`Thread.category`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.category "discord.Thread.category")
    
*   [`Thread.category_id`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.category_id "discord.Thread.category_id")
    
*   [`Thread.is_news()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.is_news "discord.Thread.is_news")
    
*   [`Thread.is_nsfw()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.is_nsfw "discord.Thread.is_nsfw")
    
*   [`Thread.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.permissions_for "discord.Thread.permissions_for")
    
    > *   Note that this outputs the permissions of the parent channel and you might need to check for different permissions when trying to determine if a member can do something.
    >     
    >     Here are some notable examples:
    >     
    >     *   A guild member can send messages in a text channel if they have [`send_messages`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages "discord.Permissions.send_messages")
    >          permission in it.
    >         
    >         A guild member can send messages in a public thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             .
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can send messages in a private thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They’re either already a member of the thread or have a [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             .
    >             
    >         
    >     *   A guild member can edit a text channel if they have [`manage_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_channels "discord.Permissions.manage_channels")
    >          permission in it.
    >         
    >         A guild member can edit a thread if they have [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >          permission in its parent channel.
    >         
    >         Note
    >         
    >         A thread’s [`owner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.owner "discord.Thread.owner")
    >          can archive a (not-locked) thread and edit its [`name`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.name "discord.Thread.name")
    >          and [`auto_archive_duration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.auto_archive_duration "discord.Thread.auto_archive_duration")
    >          without [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >          permission.
    >         
    >     *   A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a text channel if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in it.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in it or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a public thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in its parent channel or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`archived`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.archived "discord.Thread.archived")
    >             . Note that the guild member can unarchive a thread (if it’s not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             ) to react to a message.
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a private thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in its parent channel or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         *   They’re either already a member of the thread or have a [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`archived`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.archived "discord.Thread.archived")
    >             . Note that the guild member can unarchive a thread (if it’s not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             ) to react to a message.
    >             
    >         
    

The following changes have been made:

*   [`Message.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.channel "discord.Message.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Message.channel_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.channel_mentions "discord.Message.channel_mentions")
     list may now contain a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`AuditLogEntry.target`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.target "discord.AuditLogEntry.target")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`PartialMessage.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PartialMessage.channel "discord.PartialMessage.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.get_channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel "discord.Guild.get_channel")
     does not return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    s.
    
    > *   If you’re looking to get a channel or thread, use [`Guild.get_channel_or_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel_or_thread "discord.Guild.get_channel_or_thread")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   If you’re only looking to get threads, use [`Guild.get_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_thread "discord.Guild.get_thread")
    >      or [`TextChannel.get_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel.get_thread "discord.TextChannel.get_thread")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `channel` parameter in [`on_guild_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update "discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   `channel` parameter in [`on_typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_typing "discord.on_typing")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Client.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_channel "discord.Client.fetch_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Client.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.get_channel "discord.Client.get_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channel "discord.Guild.fetch_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    

Removing In-Place Edits[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#removing-in-place-edits "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Most of the model methods that previously edited the model in-place have been updated to no longer do this. Instead, these methods will now return a new instance of the newly updated model. This has been done to avoid the library running into race conditions between in-place edits and gateway events on model updates. See [GH-4098](https://github.com/dolfies/discord.py-self/issues/4098)
 for more information.

Quick example:

\# before
await member.edit(nick\='new nick')
await member.send(f'Your new nick is {member.nick}')

\# after
updated\_member \= await member.edit(nick\='new nick')
await member.send(f'Your new nick is {updated\_member.nick}')

The following have been changed:

*   [`CategoryChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel.edit "discord.CategoryChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`CategoryChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel "discord.CategoryChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method only returns the updated [`Member`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member "discord.Member")
    >      when certain fields are updated.
    >     
    
*   [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`StageChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel "discord.StageChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`TextChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel.edit "discord.TextChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`TextChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel "discord.TextChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`VoiceChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.edit "discord.VoiceChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`ClientUser.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ClientUser.edit "discord.ClientUser.edit")
    
*   [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
    
*   [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
    
*   [`Message.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.edit "discord.Message.edit")
    
*   [`Role.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Role.edit "discord.Role.edit")
    
*   [`Template.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Template.edit "discord.Template.edit")
    
*   [`Template.sync()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Template.sync "discord.Template.sync")
    
*   [`Webhook.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.edit "discord.Webhook.edit")
    
*   [`Webhook.edit_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.edit_message "discord.Webhook.edit_message")
    
*   [`WebhookMessage.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage.edit "discord.WebhookMessage.edit")
    

Sticker Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#sticker-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Discord has changed how their stickers work and as such, sticker support has been reworked.

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   Type of [`Message.stickers`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.stickers "discord.Message.stickers")
     changed to List\[[`StickerItem`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerItem "discord.StickerItem")\
    \].
    
    > *   To get the [`Sticker`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Sticker "discord.Sticker")
    >      from [`StickerItem`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerItem "discord.StickerItem")
    >     , use [`StickerItem.fetch()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerItem.fetch "discord.StickerItem.fetch")
    >      or (only for stickers from guilds the bot is in) [`Client.get_sticker()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.get_sticker "discord.Client.get_sticker")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Sticker.format`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Sticker.format "discord.Sticker.format")
     is now of [`StickerFormatType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerFormatType "discord.StickerFormatType")
     type.
    
*   `Sticker.tags` has been removed.
    
    > *   Depending on type of the sticker, [`StandardSticker.tags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StandardSticker.tags "discord.StandardSticker.tags")
    >      or [`GuildSticker.emoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.GuildSticker.emoji "discord.GuildSticker.emoji")
    >      can be used instead.
    >     
    
*   `Sticker.image` and related methods have been removed.
    
*   `Sticker.preview_image` and related methods have been removed.
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.type "discord.AuditLogDiff.type")
     is now of Union\[[`ChannelType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ChannelType "discord.ChannelType")\
    , [`StickerType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerType "discord.StickerType")\
    \] type.
    
*   The old `StickerType` enum has been renamed to [`StickerFormatType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerFormatType "discord.StickerFormatType")
    .
    
    > *   [`StickerType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StickerType "discord.StickerType")
    >      now refers to a sticker type (official sticker vs guild-uploaded sticker) rather than its format type.
    >     
    

Integrations Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#integrations-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To support the new integration types, integration support has been reworked.

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   The old `Integration` class has been renamed to [`StreamIntegration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StreamIntegration "discord.StreamIntegration")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.integrations()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.integrations "discord.Guild.integrations")
     now returns subclasses of the new [`Integration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Integration "discord.Integration")
     class.
    

Presence Updates Now Have A Separate Event[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#presence-updates-now-have-a-separate-event "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Presence updates (changes in member’s status and activity) now have a separate [`on_presence_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_presence_update "discord.on_presence_update")
 event. [`on_member_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_member_update "discord.on_member_update")
 event is now only called on member updates (changes in nickname, role, pending status, etc.).

From API perspective, these are separate events and as such, this change improves library’s consistency with the API. Presence updates usually are 90% of all handled events so splitting these should benefit listeners that were only interested in member updates.

Note that this event is also called when a [`Relationship`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Relationship "discord.Relationship")
 presence is updated.

Quick example:

\# before
@client.event
async def on\_member\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.nick != after.nick:
        await nick\_changed(before, after)
    if before.status != after.status:
        await status\_changed(before, after)

\# after
@client.event
async def on\_member\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.nick != after.nick:
        await nick\_changed(before, after)

@client.event
async def on\_presence\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.status != after.status:
        await status\_changed(before, after)

Moving Away From Custom AsyncIterator[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#moving-away-from-custom-asynciterator "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Asynchronous iterators in v1.0 were implemented using a special class named `AsyncIterator`. v2.0 instead provides regular asynchronous iterators with no added utility methods.

This means that usage of the following utility methods is no longer possible:

*   `AsyncIterator.next()`
    
    > Usage of an explicit `async for` loop should generally be preferred:
    > 
    > \# before
    > it \= channel.history()
    > while True:
    >     try:
    >         message \= await self.next()
    >     except discord.NoMoreItems:
    >         break
    >     print(f'Found message with ID {message.id}')
    > 
    > \# after
    > async for message in channel.history():
    >     print(f'Found message with ID {message.id}')
    > 
    > If you need to get next item from an iterator without a loop, you can use [`anext()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#anext "(in Python v3.14)")
    >  (new in Python 3.10) or [`__anext__()`](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__anext__ "(in Python v3.14)")
    >  instead:
    > 
    > \# before
    > it \= channel.history()
    > first \= await it.next()
    > if first.content \== 'do not iterate':
    >     return
    > async for message in it:
    >     ...
    > 
    > \# after
    > it \= channel.history()
    > first \= await anext(it)  \# await it.\_\_anext\_\_() on Python<3.10
    > if first.content \== 'do not iterate':
    >     return
    > async for message in it:
    >     ...
    
*   `AsyncIterator.get()`
    
    > \# before
    > msg \= await channel.history().get(author\_\_name\='Dave')
    > 
    > \# after
    > msg \= await discord.utils.get(channel.history(), author\_\_name\='Dave')
    
*   `AsyncIterator.find()`
    
    > def predicate(event):
    >     return event.reason is not None
    > 
    > \# before
    > event \= await guild.audit\_logs().find(predicate)
    > 
    > \# after
    > event \= await discord.utils.find(predicate, guild.audit\_logs())
    
*   `AsyncIterator.flatten()`
    
    > \# before
    > users \= await reaction.users().flatten()
    > 
    > \# after
    > users \= \[user async for user in reaction.users()\]
    
*   `AsyncIterator.chunk()`
    
    > \# before
    > async for leader, \*users in reaction.users().chunk(3):
    >     ...
    > 
    > \# after
    > async for leader, \*users in discord.utils.as\_chunks(reaction.users(), 3):
    >     ...
    
*   `AsyncIterator.map()`
    
    > \# before
    > content\_of\_messages \= \[\]
    > async for content in channel.history().map(lambda m: m.content):
    >     content\_of\_messages.append(content)
    > 
    > \# after
    > content\_of\_messages \= \[message.content async for message in channel.history()\]
    
*   `AsyncIterator.filter()`
    
    > def predicate(message):
    >     return not message.author.bot
    > 
    > \# before
    > user\_messages \= \[\]
    > async for message in channel.history().filter(lambda m: not m.author.bot):
    >     user\_messages.append(message)
    > 
    > \# after
    > user\_messages \= \[message async for message in channel.history() if not m.author.bot\]
    

To ease this transition, these changes have been made:

*   Added [`utils.as_chunks()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.as_chunks "discord.utils.as_chunks")
     as an alternative for `AsyncIter.chunk`.
    
*   Added support for [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
     to [`utils.find()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.find "discord.utils.find")
    .
    
*   Added support for [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
     to [`utils.get()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.get "discord.utils.get")
    .
    

The return type of the following methods has been changed to an [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
:

*   [`abc.Messageable.history()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.history "discord.abc.Messageable.history")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guilds()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guilds "discord.Client.fetch_guilds")
    
*   [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_members "discord.Guild.fetch_members")
    
*   [`Reaction.users()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Reaction.users "discord.Reaction.users")
    

The `NoMoreItems` exception was removed as calling [`anext()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#anext "(in Python v3.14)")
 or [`__anext__()`](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__anext__ "(in Python v3.14)")
 on an [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
 will now raise [`StopAsyncIteration`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#StopAsyncIteration "(in Python v3.14)")
.

Changing certain lists to be lazy sequences instead[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#changing-certain-lists-to-be-lazy-sequences-instead "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to improve performance when calculating the length of certain lists, certain attributes were changed to return a sequence rather than a [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
.

A sequence is similar to a [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
 except it is read-only. In order to get a list again you can call [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
 on the resulting sequence.

The following properties were changed to return a sequence instead of a list:

*   [`Client.guilds`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.guilds "discord.Client.guilds")
    
*   [`Client.emojis`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.emojis "discord.Client.emojis")
    
*   [`Client.private_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.private_channels "discord.Client.private_channels")
    
*   [`Guild.roles`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.roles "discord.Guild.roles")
    
*   [`Guild.channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.channels "discord.Guild.channels")
    
*   [`Guild.members`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.members "discord.Guild.members")
    

This change should be transparent, unless you are modifying the sequence by doing things such as `list.append`.

Embed Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#embed-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Originally, embeds used a special sentinel to denote emptiness or remove an attribute from display. The `Embed.Empty` sentinel was made when Discord’s embed design was in a nebulous state of flux. Since then, the embed design has stabilised and thus the sentinel is seen as legacy.

Therefore, `Embed.Empty` has been removed in favour of `None`.

Additionally, `Embed.__eq__` has been implemented thus embeds becoming unhashable (e.g. using them in sets or dict keys).

\# before
embed \= discord.Embed(title\='foo')
embed.title \= discord.Embed.Empty
embed \== embed.copy() \# False

\# after
embed \= discord.Embed(title\='foo')
embed.title \= None
embed \== embed.copy() \# True
{embed, embed} \# Raises TypeError

Removal of `InvalidArgument` Exception[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#removal-of-invalidargument-exception "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The custom `InvalidArgument` exception has been removed and functions and methods that raised it are now raising [`TypeError`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)")
 and/or [`ValueError`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)")
 instead.

The following methods have been changed:

*   [`Message.add_reaction()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.add_reaction "discord.Message.add_reaction")
    
*   `AutoShardedClient.change_presence()`
    
*   [`Client.change_presence()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.change_presence "discord.Client.change_presence")
    
*   [`Reaction.clear()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Reaction.clear "discord.Reaction.clear")

*   [`Guild.create_custom_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji "discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji")
    
*   [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   [`Template.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Template.create_guild "discord.Template.create_guild")

Logging Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#logging-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The library now provides a default logging configuration if using [`Client.run()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.run "discord.Client.run")
. To disable it, pass `None` to the `log_handler` keyword parameter. Since the library now provides a default logging configuration, certain methods were changed to no longer print to [`sys.stderr`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/sys.html#sys.stderr "(in Python v3.14)")
 but use the logger instead:

*   [`Client.on_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.on_error "discord.Client.on_error")
    
*   [`discord.ext.tasks.Loop.error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.error "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.error")
    
*   [`discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error "discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error")
    
*   [`VoiceClient.play()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceClient.play "discord.VoiceClient.play")
    

For more information, check [Setting Up Logging](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/logging.html)
.

Text in Voice[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#text-in-voice "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to support text in voice functionality, a few changes had to be made:

*   [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
     is now [`abc.Messageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable "discord.abc.Messageable")
     so it can have messages sent and received.
    
*   [`Message.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.channel "discord.Message.channel")
     can now be [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
     and [`StageChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel "discord.StageChannel")
    .
    

Removal of `StoreChannel`[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#removal-of-storechannel "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Discord’s API has removed store channels as of [March 10th, 2022](https://support-dev.discord.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309018858647)
. Therefore, the library has removed support for it as well.

This removes the following:

*   `StoreChannel`
    
*   `commands.StoreChannelConverter`
    

Change in `Guild.bans` endpoint[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#change-in-guild-bans-endpoint "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Due to a breaking API change by Discord, [`Guild.bans()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.bans "discord.Guild.bans")
 no longer returns a list of every ban in the guild but instead is paginated using an asynchronous iterator.

\# before

bans \= await guild.bans()

\# after
async for ban in guild.bans(limit\=1000):
    ...

Flag classes now have a custom `bool()` implementation[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#flag-classes-now-have-a-custom-bool-implementation "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To allow library users to easily check whether an instance of a flag class has any flags enabled, using bool on them will now only return `True` if at least one flag is enabled.

This means that evaluating instances of the following classes in a bool context (such as `if obj:`) may no longer return `True`:

*   [`MemberCacheFlags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.MemberCacheFlags "discord.MemberCacheFlags")
    
*   [`MessageFlags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.MessageFlags "discord.MessageFlags")
    
*   [`Permissions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions "discord.Permissions")
    
*   [`PublicUserFlags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PublicUserFlags "discord.PublicUserFlags")
    
*   [`SystemChannelFlags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.SystemChannelFlags "discord.SystemChannelFlags")
    

Function Signature Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#function-signature-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Parameters in the following methods are now all positional-only:

*   [`Client.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.get_channel "discord.Client.get_channel")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_channel "discord.Client.fetch_channel")
    
*   [`Guild.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel "discord.Guild.get_channel")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channel "discord.Guild.fetch_channel")
    
*   [`Client.get_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.get_emoji "discord.Client.get_emoji")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_emoji "discord.Guild.fetch_emoji")
    
*   [`Client.get_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.get_guild "discord.Client.get_guild")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guild "discord.Client.fetch_guild")
    
*   [`Client.delete_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.delete_invite "discord.Client.delete_invite")
    
*   [`Guild.get_member()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_member "discord.Guild.get_member")
    
*   [`Guild.get_member_named()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_member_named "discord.Guild.get_member_named")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_member()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_member "discord.Guild.fetch_member")
    
*   [`Client.get_user()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.get_user "discord.Client.get_user")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_user()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_user "discord.Client.fetch_user")
    
*   [`Guild.get_role()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.get_role "discord.Guild.get_role")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_webhook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_webhook "discord.Client.fetch_webhook")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_widget()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_widget "discord.Client.fetch_widget")
    
*   [`Message.add_reaction()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.add_reaction "discord.Message.add_reaction")
    
*   [`Client.on_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.on_error "discord.Client.on_error")
    
*   [`abc.Messageable.fetch_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.fetch_message "discord.abc.Messageable.fetch_message")
    
*   [`abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for "discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for")
    
*   [`DMChannel.get_partial_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.DMChannel.get_partial_message "discord.DMChannel.get_partial_message")
    
*   [`TextChannel.get_partial_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel.get_partial_message "discord.TextChannel.get_partial_message")
    
*   [`TextChannel.delete_messages()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel.delete_messages "discord.TextChannel.delete_messages")
    
*   [`Webhook.delete_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.delete_message "discord.Webhook.delete_message")
    
*   [`utils.find()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.find "discord.utils.find")
    
*   [`utils.snowflake_time()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.snowflake_time "discord.utils.snowflake_time")
    

The following parameters are now positional-only:

*   `iterable` in [`utils.get()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.get "discord.utils.get")
    
*   `event_method` in [`Client.on_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.on_error "discord.Client.on_error")
    
*   `event` in [`Client.wait_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.wait_for "discord.Client.wait_for")
    
*   `dt` in [`utils.time_snowflake()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.time_snowflake "discord.utils.time_snowflake")
    

The following are now keyword-only:

*   Parameters in [`Reaction.users()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Reaction.users "discord.Reaction.users")
    
*   Parameters in [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   `permissions`, `guild`, `redirect_uri`, and `scopes` parameters in [`utils.oauth_url()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.oauth_url "discord.utils.oauth_url")
    
*   `high` in [`utils.snowflake_time()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.snowflake_time "discord.utils.snowflake_time")
    

The library now less often uses `None` as the default value for function/method parameters.

As a result, these parameters can no longer be `None`:

*   `size`, `format`, and `static_format` in [`Asset.replace()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    
*   `check` in [`TextChannel.purge()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.TextChannel.purge "discord.TextChannel.purge")
    
*   `icon` and `code` in [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   `roles` in [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
    
*   `topic`, `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_text_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_text_channel "discord.Guild.create_text_channel")
    
*   `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_voice_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_voice_channel "discord.Guild.create_voice_channel")
    
*   `topic`, `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_stage_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_stage_channel "discord.Guild.create_stage_channel")
    
*   `position` and `overwrites` in [`Guild.create_category()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_category "discord.Guild.create_category")
    
*   `roles` in [`Guild.prune_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.prune_members "discord.Guild.prune_members")
    
*   `roles` in [`Guild.estimate_pruned_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.estimate_pruned_members "discord.Guild.estimate_pruned_members")
    
*   `description` in [`Guild.create_template()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_template "discord.Guild.create_template")
    
*   `roles` in [`Guild.create_custom_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji "discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji")
    
*   `before`, `after`, `oldest_first`, `user`, and `action` in [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
    
*   `enable_emoticons` in [`StreamIntegration.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StreamIntegration.edit "discord.StreamIntegration.edit")
    
*   `mute`, `deafen`, `suppress`, and `roles` in [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
*   `position` in [`Role.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Role.edit "discord.Role.edit")
    
*   `icon` in [`Template.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Template.create_guild "discord.Template.create_guild")
    
*   `name` in [`Template.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Template.edit "discord.Template.edit")
    
*   `permissions`, `guild`, `redirect_uri`, `scopes` in [`utils.oauth_url()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.oauth_url "discord.utils.oauth_url")
    
*   `content`, `username`, `avatar_url`, `tts`, `file`, `files`, `embed`, `embeds`, and `allowed_mentions` in [`Webhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.send "discord.Webhook.send")
    

Allowed types for the following parameters have been changed:

*   `rtc_region` in [`Guild.create_voice_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.create_voice_channel "discord.Guild.create_voice_channel")
     is now of type Optional\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")\
    \].
    
*   `rtc_region` in [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
     is now of type Optional\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")\
    \].
    
*   `rtc_region` in [`VoiceChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.edit "discord.VoiceChannel.edit")
     is now of type Optional\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")\
    \].
    
*   `preferred_locale` in [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
     is now of type [`Locale`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Locale "discord.Locale")
    .
    

Attribute Type Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#attribute-type-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following changes have been made:

*   [`DMChannel.recipient`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.DMChannel.recipient "discord.DMChannel.recipient")
     may now be `None`.
    
*   [`Guild.vanity_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.vanity_invite "discord.Guild.vanity_invite")
     may now be `None`. This has been done to fix an issue with the method returning a broken [`Invite`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Invite "discord.Invite")
     object.
    
*   [`Widget.fetch_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Widget.fetch_invite "discord.Widget.fetch_invite")
     may now be `None`.
    
*   [`Guild.mfa_level`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.mfa_level "discord.Guild.mfa_level")
     is now of type [`MFALevel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.MFALevel "discord.MFALevel")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.member_count`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.member_count "discord.Guild.member_count")
     is now of type Optional\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.14)")\
    \].
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.mfa_level`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.mfa_level "discord.AuditLogDiff.mfa_level")
     is now of type [`MFALevel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.MFALevel "discord.MFALevel")
    .
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.rtc_region`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.rtc_region "discord.AuditLogDiff.rtc_region")
     is now of type [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    .
    
*   [`StageChannel.rtc_region`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel.rtc_region "discord.StageChannel.rtc_region")
     is now of type [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    .
    
*   [`VoiceChannel.rtc_region`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.rtc_region "discord.VoiceChannel.rtc_region")
     is now of type [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    .
    
*   [`ClientUser.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ClientUser.avatar "discord.ClientUser.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a user has displayed, consider [`ClientUser.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ClientUser.display_avatar "discord.ClientUser.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Member.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member.avatar "discord.Member.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a member or user has displayed, consider [`Member.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Member.display_avatar "discord.Member.display_avatar")
    >      or [`User.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.User.display_avatar "discord.User.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`User.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.User.avatar "discord.User.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a user has displayed, consider [`User.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.User.display_avatar "discord.User.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Webhook.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.avatar "discord.Webhook.avatar")
     is now `None` when the default avatar is used.
    
    > *   If you want the avatar that a webhook has displayed, consider [`Webhook.display_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Webhook.display_avatar "discord.Webhook.display_avatar")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`AuditLogEntry.target`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.target "discord.AuditLogEntry.target")
     may now be a [`PartialMessageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PartialMessageable "discord.PartialMessageable")
    .
    
*   [`PartialMessage.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PartialMessage.channel "discord.PartialMessage.channel")
     may now be a [`PartialMessageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PartialMessageable "discord.PartialMessageable")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.preferred_locale`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.preferred_locale "discord.Guild.preferred_locale")
     is now of type [`Locale`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Locale "discord.Locale")
    .
    
*   [`abc.GuildChannel.overwrites`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.overwrites "discord.abc.GuildChannel.overwrites")
     keys can now have [`Object`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Object "discord.Object")
     in them.
    

Removals[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#removals "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following deprecated functionality have been removed:

*   `Client.request_offline_members`
    
    > *   Use [`Guild.chunk()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.chunk "discord.Guild.chunk")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `AutoShardedClient.request_offline_members`
    
    > *   Use [`Guild.chunk()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.chunk "discord.Guild.chunk")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `Client.logout`
    
    > *   Use [`Client.close()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.close "discord.Client.close")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `fetch_offline_members` parameter from [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
     constructor
    
    > *   Use `chunk_guild_at_startup` instead.
    >     
    
*   `Permissions.use_slash_commands` and `PermissionOverwrite.use_slash_commands`
    
    *   Use [`Permissions.use_application_commands`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.use_application_commands "discord.Permissions.use_application_commands")
         and `PermissionOverwrite.use_application_commands` instead.
        
    

The following have been removed:

*   `MemberCacheFlags.online`
    
    > *   There is no replacement for this one. The current API version no longer provides enough data for this to be possible.
    >     
    
*   `User.permissions_in` and `Member.permissions_in`
    
    > *   Use [`abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for "discord.abc.GuildChannel.permissions_for")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `guild_subscriptions` parameter from [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
     constructor
    
    > *   The current API version no longer provides this functionality.
    >     
    
*   `guild_subscription_options` parameter from [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
     constructor
    
    > *   The auto-subscribing system has been removed. `chunk_guilds_at_startup` is now utilised properly.
    >     
    
*   [`VerificationLevel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel "discord.VerificationLevel")
     aliases:
    
    > *   `VerificationLevel.table_flip` - use [`VerificationLevel.high`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.high "discord.VerificationLevel.high")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   `VerificationLevel.extreme` - use [`VerificationLevel.highest`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.highest "discord.VerificationLevel.highest")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   `VerificationLevel.double_table_flip` - use [`VerificationLevel.highest`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.highest "discord.VerificationLevel.highest")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   `VerificationLevel.very_high` - use [`VerificationLevel.highest`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VerificationLevel.highest "discord.VerificationLevel.highest")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `topic` parameter from [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
    
    > *   The `topic` parameter must now be set via [`StageChannel.create_instance()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.StageChannel.create_instance "discord.StageChannel.create_instance")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   `Reaction.custom_emoji`
    
    > *   Use [`Reaction.is_custom_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Reaction.is_custom_emoji "discord.Reaction.is_custom_emoji")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `AuditLogDiff.region`
    
*   `Guild.region`
    
*   `VoiceRegion`
    
    > *   This has been marked deprecated by Discord and it was usually more or less out of date due to the pace they added them anyway.
    >     
    
*   `region` parameter from [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   `region` parameter from [`Template.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Template.create_guild "discord.Template.create_guild")
    
*   `region` parameter from [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
    
*   The undocumented private `on_socket_response` event
    
    > *   Consider using the newer documented [`on_socket_event_type()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_socket_event_type "discord.on_socket_event_type")
    >      event instead.
    >     
    
*   `abc.Messageable.trigger_typing`
    
    > *   Use [`abc.Messageable.typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.typing "discord.abc.Messageable.typing")
    >      with `await` instead.
    >     
    

Miscellaneous Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#miscellaneous-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following changes have been made:

*   [`on_socket_raw_receive()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_socket_raw_receive "discord.on_socket_raw_receive")
     is now only called if `enable_debug_events` is set on [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
    .
    
*   [`on_socket_raw_receive()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_socket_raw_receive "discord.on_socket_raw_receive")
     is now only called once the **complete** message is received and decompressed. The passed `msg` parameter is now always [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    .
    
*   [`on_socket_raw_send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.on_socket_raw_send "discord.on_socket_raw_send")
     is now only called if `enable_debug_events` is set on [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
    .
    
*   The documented return type for [`Guild.fetch_channels()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channels "discord.Guild.fetch_channels")
     changed to Sequence\[[`abc.GuildChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel "discord.abc.GuildChannel")\
    \].
    
*   [`utils.resolve_invite()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.resolve_invite "discord.utils.resolve_invite")
     now returns a [`ResolvedInvite`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ResolvedInvite "discord.ResolvedInvite")
     class.
    
*   [`utils.oauth_url()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.utils.oauth_url "discord.utils.oauth_url")
     now defaults to `bot` and `applications.commands` scopes when not given instead of just `bot`.
    
*   [`abc.Messageable.typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.typing "discord.abc.Messageable.typing")
     can no longer be used as a regular (non-async) context manager.
    
*   `created_at` is no longer part of [`abc.Snowflake`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.abc.Snowflake "discord.abc.Snowflake")
    .
    
    > All of the existing classes still keep this attribute. It is just no longer part of this protocol. This has been done because Discord reuses IDs (snowflakes) of some models in other models. For example, if [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >  is created from a message, its [`Thread.id`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.id "discord.Thread.id")
    >  is equivalent to the ID of that message and as such it doesn’t contain information about creation time of the thread and [`Thread.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread.created_at "discord.Thread.created_at")
    >  cannot be based on it.
    
*   [`Embed`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Embed "discord.Embed")
    ’s bool implementation now returns `True` when embed has any data set.
    
*   Calling [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
     without `roles` argument no longer makes the emoji available to everyone.
    
    > *   To make the emoji available to everyone, pass an empty list to `roles` instead.
    >     
    
*   The old `Colour.blurple` has been renamed to [`Colour.og_blurple`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Colour.og_blurple "discord.Colour.og_blurple")
    .
    
    > *   [`Colour.blurple`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Colour.blurple "discord.Colour.blurple")
    >      refers to a different colour now.
    >     
    
*   [`Message.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.type "discord.Message.type")
     is now set to [`MessageType.reply`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.MessageType.reply "discord.MessageType.reply")
     when a message is a reply.
    
    > *   This is caused by a difference in behavior in the current Discord API version.
    >     
    
*   [`Message.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message.edit "discord.Message.edit")
     now merges object passed in `allowed_mentions` parameter with [`Client.allowed_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.allowed_mentions "discord.Client.allowed_mentions")
    . If the parameter isn’t provided, the defaults given by [`Client.allowed_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Client.allowed_mentions "discord.Client.allowed_mentions")
     are used instead.
    
*   [`Permissions.stage_moderator()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.stage_moderator "discord.Permissions.stage_moderator")
     now includes the [`Permissions.manage_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_channels "discord.Permissions.manage_channels")
     permission and the [`Permissions.request_to_speak`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Permissions.request_to_speak "discord.Permissions.request_to_speak")
     permission is no longer included.
    
*   [`File.filename`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.File.filename "discord.File.filename")
     will no longer be `None`, in situations where previously this was the case the filename is set to `'untitled'`.
    

[`VoiceProtocol.connect()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceProtocol.connect "discord.VoiceProtocol.connect")
 signature changes.[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#voiceprotocol-connect-signature-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`VoiceProtocol.connect()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.VoiceProtocol.connect "discord.VoiceProtocol.connect")
 will now be passed 2 keyword only arguments, `self_deaf` and `self_mute`. These indicate whether or not the client should join the voice chat being deafened or muted.

Command Extension Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#command-extension-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#extension-and-cog-loading-unloading-is-now-asynchronous "Permalink to this headline")

As an extension to the [asyncio changes](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-client-async-setup)
 the loading and unloading of extensions and cogs is now asynchronous.

To accommodate this, the following changes have been made:

*   The `setup` and `teardown` functions in extensions must now be coroutines.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.load_extension()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.load_extension "discord.ext.commands.Bot.load_extension")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.unload_extension()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.unload_extension "discord.ext.commands.Bot.unload_extension")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.reload_extension()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.reload_extension "discord.ext.commands.Bot.reload_extension")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.add_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog")
     must now be awaited.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog")
     must now be awaited.
    

Quick example of an extension setup function:

\# before
def setup(bot):
    bot.add\_cog(MyCog(bot))

\# after
async def setup(bot):
    await bot.add\_cog(MyCog(bot))

Quick example of loading an extension:

\# before
bot.load\_extension('my\_extension')

\# after using setup\_hook
class MyBot(commands.Bot):
    async def setup\_hook(self):
        await self.load\_extension('my\_extension')

\# after using async\_with
async def main():
    async with bot:
        await bot.load\_extension('my\_extension')
        await bot.start(TOKEN)

asyncio.run(main())

### Converters Are Now Generic Runtime Protocols[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#converters-are-now-generic-runtime-protocols "Permalink to this headline")

[`Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")
 is now a [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.14)")
 [`typing.Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
.

This results in a change of the base metaclass used by these classes which may affect user-created classes that inherit from [`Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")
.

Quick example:

\# before
class SomeConverterMeta(type):
    ...

class SomeConverter(commands.Converter, metaclass\=SomeConverterMeta):
    ...

\# after
class SomeConverterMeta(type(commands.Converter)):
    ...

class SomeConverter(commands.Converter, metaclass\=SomeConverterMeta):
    ...

In addition, [`Converter`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Converter "discord.ext.commands.Converter")
 is now a [`typing.Generic`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generic "(in Python v3.14)")
 which (optionally) allows the users to define their type hints more accurately.

### Function Signature Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#id1 "Permalink to this headline")

Parameters in the following methods are now all positional-only:

*   [`ext.commands.when_mentioned()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.when_mentioned "discord.ext.commands.when_mentioned")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error "discord.ext.commands.Bot.on_command_error")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.check")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.check_once()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.check_once "discord.ext.commands.Bot.check_once")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.is_owner()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.is_owner "discord.ext.commands.Bot.is_owner")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.before_invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.before_invoke "discord.ext.commands.Bot.before_invoke")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.after_invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.after_invoke "discord.ext.commands.Bot.after_invoke")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.get_prefix()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_prefix "discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_prefix")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.invoke "discord.ext.commands.Bot.invoke")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Bot.process_commands()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.process_commands "discord.ext.commands.Bot.process_commands")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.is_on_cooldown()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.is_on_cooldown "discord.ext.commands.Command.is_on_cooldown")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.reset_cooldown()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.reset_cooldown "discord.ext.commands.Command.reset_cooldown")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.get_cooldown_retry_after()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.get_cooldown_retry_after "discord.ext.commands.Command.get_cooldown_retry_after")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.error()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.error "discord.ext.commands.Command.error")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.before_invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.before_invoke "discord.ext.commands.Command.before_invoke")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.after_invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.after_invoke "discord.ext.commands.Command.after_invoke")
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.can_run()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.can_run "discord.ext.commands.Command.can_run")
    
*   [`ext.commands.check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.check "discord.ext.commands.check")
    
*   [`ext.commands.has_role()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.has_role "discord.ext.commands.has_role")
    
*   [`ext.commands.bot_has_role()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.bot_has_role "discord.ext.commands.bot_has_role")
    
*   [`ext.commands.before_invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.before_invoke "discord.ext.commands.before_invoke")
