The following parameters are now positional-only:

*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_check "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.check_once()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.check_once "discord.ext.commands.Bot.check_once")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener")
    
*   `message` in [`ext.commands.Bot.get_context()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_context "discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_context")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Command.add_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.add_check "discord.ext.commands.Command.add_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.Command.remove_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.remove_check "discord.ext.commands.Command.remove_check")
    
*   `context` in [`ext.commands.Command.__call__()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.__call__ "discord.ext.commands.Command.__call__")
    
*   `commands` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.filter_commands()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.filter_commands "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.filter_commands")
    
*   `ctx` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.command_callback()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.command_callback "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.command_callback")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.add_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.add_check "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.add_check")
    
*   `func` in [`ext.commands.HelpCommand.remove_check()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.remove_check "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand.remove_check")
    
*   `commands` in [`ext.commands.DefaultHelpCommand.add_indented_commands()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.DefaultHelpCommand.add_indented_commands "discord.ext.commands.DefaultHelpCommand.add_indented_commands")
    
*   `cog` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.get_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.get_cog")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_cog")
    
*   `command` in [`ext.commands.Context.invoke()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.invoke "discord.ext.commands.Context.invoke")
    
*   `command` in [`ext.commands.GroupMixin.add_command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.add_command "discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.add_command")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.GroupMixin.get_command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.get_command "discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.get_command")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.GroupMixin.remove_command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.remove_command "discord.ext.commands.GroupMixin.remove_command")
    

The following parameters have been added:

*   `user_bot` in [`Bot`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot "discord.ext.commands.Bot")
    
    > *   Allows your bot to reply to everyone’s message.
    >     
    

The library now less often uses `None` as the default value for function/method parameters.

As a result, these parameters can no longer be `None`:

*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.add_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_listener")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener "discord.ext.commands.Bot.remove_listener")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Bot.listen()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.listen "discord.ext.commands.Bot.listen")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Cog.listener()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cog.listener "discord.ext.commands.Cog.listener")
    
*   `name` in [`ext.commands.Command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command "discord.ext.commands.Command")
    
*   `name` and `cls` in [`ext.commands.command()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.command "discord.ext.commands.command")
    
*   `name` and `cls` in [`ext.commands.group()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.group "discord.ext.commands.group")
    

### Removals[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#id2 "Permalink to this headline")

The following attributes have been removed:

*   `original` from the [`ExtensionNotFound`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound "discord.ext.commands.ExtensionNotFound")
    
*   `type` from the [`Cooldown`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cooldown "discord.ext.commands.Cooldown")
     class that was provided by the [`ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.cooldown`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.cooldown "discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.cooldown")
     attribute
    
    > *   Use [`ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.type "discord.ext.commands.CommandOnCooldown.type")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `clean_prefix` from the [`HelpCommand`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand "discord.ext.commands.HelpCommand")
    
    > *   Use [`ext.commands.Context.clean_prefix`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.clean_prefix "discord.ext.commands.Context.clean_prefix")
    >      instead.
    >     
    

### Miscellaneous Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#id3 "Permalink to this headline")

*   [`ext.commands.Bot.add_cog()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog "discord.ext.commands.Bot.add_cog")
     is now raising [`ClientException`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.ClientException "discord.ClientException")
     when a cog with the same name is already loaded.
    
    > *   To override a cog, the new `override` parameter can be used.
    >     
    
*   When passing a callable to `type` argument of [`cooldown()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.cooldown "discord.ext.commands.cooldown")
    , it now needs to accept [`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
     rather than [`Message`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Message "discord.Message")
     as its only argument.
    
*   Metaclass of [`Context`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context "discord.ext.commands.Context")
     changed from [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.14)")
     to [`type`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.14)")
    .
    
*   Changed type of [`ext.commands.Command.clean_params`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params "discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params")
     from [`collections.OrderedDict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.html#collections.OrderedDict "(in Python v3.14)")
     to [`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.14)")
    . as the latter is guaranteed to preserve insertion order since Python 3.7.
    
*   [`ext.commands.ChannelNotReadable.argument`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.ChannelNotReadable.argument "discord.ext.commands.ChannelNotReadable.argument")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
     due to the [Thread Support](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-thread-support)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.NSFWChannelRequired.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.NSFWChannelRequired.channel "discord.ext.commands.NSFWChannelRequired.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
     due to the [Thread Support](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-thread-support)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Context.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.channel "discord.ext.commands.Context.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
     due to the [Thread Support](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-thread-support)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Context.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Context.channel "discord.ext.commands.Context.channel")
     may now be a [`PartialMessageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#discord.PartialMessageable "discord.PartialMessageable")
    .
    
*   `MissingPermissions.missing_perms` has been renamed to [`ext.commands.MissingPermissions.missing_permissions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.MissingPermissions.missing_permissions "discord.ext.commands.MissingPermissions.missing_permissions")
    .
    
*   `BotMissingPermissions.missing_perms` has been renamed to [`ext.commands.BotMissingPermissions.missing_permissions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.BotMissingPermissions.missing_permissions "discord.ext.commands.BotMissingPermissions.missing_permissions")
    .
    
*   [`ext.commands.Cog.cog_load()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_load "discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_load")
     has been added as part of the [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Cog.cog_unload()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_unload "discord.ext.commands.Cog.cog_unload")
     may now be a [coroutine](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-coroutine "(in Python v3.14)")
     due to the [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
     changes.
    
*   [`ext.commands.Command.clean_params`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/commands/api.html#discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params "discord.ext.commands.Command.clean_params")
     type now uses a custom [`inspect.Parameter`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/inspect.html#inspect.Parameter "(in Python v3.14)")
     to handle defaults.
    

Tasks Extension Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/migrating.html#tasks-extension-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Calling [`ext.tasks.Loop.stop()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.stop "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.stop")
     in [`before_loop()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.before_loop "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.before_loop")
     now stops the first iteration from running.
    
*   Calling [`ext.tasks.Loop.change_interval()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.Loop.change_interval "discord.ext.tasks.Loop.change_interval")
     now changes the interval for the sleep time right away, rather than on the next loop iteration.
    
*   `loop` parameter in [`ext.tasks.loop()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ext/tasks/index.html#discord.ext.tasks.loop "discord.ext.tasks.loop")
     can no longer be `None`.

# Version Guarantees

View Documentation For discord discord.ext.commands discord.ext.tasks

 search

settings

Version Guarantees[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/version_guarantees.html#version-guarantees "Permalink to this headline")

===============================================================================================================================================

The library follows a [semantic versioning principle](https://semver.org/)
 which means that the major version is updated every time there is an incompatible API change. However due to the lack of guarantees on the Discord side when it comes to breaking changes along with the fairly dynamic nature of Python it can be hard to discern what can be considered a breaking change and what isn’t.

The first thing to keep in mind is that breaking changes only apply to **publicly documented functions and classes**. If it’s not listed in the documentation here then it is not part of the public API and is thus bound to change. This includes attributes that start with an underscore or functions without an underscore that are not documented.

However, the Discord user API is in constant flux, so sometimes breaking changes may creep in.

Note

The examples below are non-exhaustive.

Examples of Breaking Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/version_guarantees.html#examples-of-breaking-changes "Permalink to this headline")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Changing the default parameter value to something else.
    
*   Renaming a function without an alias to an old function.
    
*   Adding or removing parameters to an event.
    

Examples of Non-Breaking Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/version_guarantees.html#examples-of-non-breaking-changes "Permalink to this headline")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Adding or removing private underscored attributes.
    
*   Adding an element into the `__slots__` of a data class.
    
*   Changing the behaviour of a function to fix a bug.
    
*   Changes in the documentation.
    
*   Modifying the internal HTTP handling.
    
*   Upgrading the dependencies to a new version, major or otherwise.
    

arrow\_upward to top

---

# Migrating to v2.0

View Documentation For discord discord.ext.commands discord.ext.tasks

 search

settings

Migrating to v2.0[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#migrating-to-v2-0 "Permalink to this headline")

====================================================================================================================================

Compared to v1.0, v2.0 mostly has breaking changes related to better developer experience and API coverage. While the changes aren’t as massive to require an entire rewrite, there are still many changes that need to be accounted for.

This is the first version to have version guarantees apply.

Below is a **non-exhaustive** list of changes.

Python Version Change[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#python-version-change "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to ease development, maintain security updates, and use newer features **v2.0 drops support for Python 3.7 and earlier**.

asyncio Event Loop Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#asyncio-event-loop-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Python 3.7 introduced a new helper function [`asyncio.run()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-runner.html#asyncio.run "(in Python v3.14)")
 which automatically creates and destroys the asynchronous event loop.

In order to support this, the way discord.py-self handles the [`asyncio`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio.html#module-asyncio "(in Python v3.14)")
 event loop has changed.

This allows you to rather than using [`Client.run()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.run "discord.Client.run")
 create your own asynchronous loop to setup other asynchronous code as needed.

Quick example:

client \= discord.Client()

async def main():
    \# do other async things
    await my\_async\_function()

    \# start the client
    async with client:
        await client.start(TOKEN)

asyncio.run(main())

A new [`setup_hook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.setup_hook "discord.Client.setup_hook")
 method has also been added to the [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 class. This method is called after login but before connecting to the discord gateway.

It is intended to be used to setup various bot features in an asynchronous context.

[`setup_hook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.setup_hook "discord.Client.setup_hook")
 can be defined by subclassing the [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 class.

Quick example:

class MyClient(discord.Client):
    async def setup\_hook(self):
        print('This is asynchronous!')

client \= MyClient()
client.run(TOKEN)

With this change, constructor of [`Client`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client "discord.Client")
 no longer accepts `connector` and `loop` parameters.

In parallel with this change, changes were made to loading and unloading of commands extension extensions and cogs, see [Extension and Cog Loading / Unloading is Now Asynchronous](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#migrating-2-0-commands-extension-cog-async)
 for more information.

Abstract Base Classes Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#abstract-base-classes-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Abstract Base Classes](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord-api-abcs)
 that inherited from [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.14)")
 now inherit from [`typing.Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
.

This results in a change of the base metaclass used by these classes but this should generally be completely transparent to the user.

All of the classes are either [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.14)")
 protocols or explicitly inherited from and as such usage with [`isinstance()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#isinstance "(in Python v3.14)")
 and [`issubclass()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#issubclass "(in Python v3.14)")
 is not affected.

The following have been changed to [`runtime-checkable`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.runtime_checkable "(in Python v3.14)")
 [`Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
s:

*   [`abc.Snowflake`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.Snowflake "discord.abc.Snowflake")
    
*   [`abc.User`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.User "discord.abc.User")
    

The following have been changed to subclass [`Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol "(in Python v3.14)")
:

*   [`abc.GuildChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel "discord.abc.GuildChannel")
    
*   [`abc.Connectable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.Connectable "discord.abc.Connectable")
    

The following have been changed to use the default metaclass instead of [`abc.ABCMeta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/abc.html#abc.ABCMeta "(in Python v3.14)")
:

*   [`abc.Messageable`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable "discord.abc.Messageable")
    
*   [`abc.PrivateChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.PrivateChannel "discord.abc.PrivateChannel")
    

`datetime` Objects Are Now UTC-Aware[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#datetime-objects-are-now-utc-aware "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All usage of naive [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects in the library has been replaced with aware objects using UTC timezone. Methods that accepted naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects now also accept timezone-aware objects. To keep behavior inline with [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
’s methods, this library’s methods now assume that naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects are local time (note that some of the methods may not accept naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
, such exceptions are listed below).

Because naive [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects are treated by many of its methods as local times, the previous behavior was more likely to result in programming errors with their usage.

To ease the migration [`utils.utcnow()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.utcnow "discord.utils.utcnow")
 helper function has been added.

Warning

Using [`datetime.datetime.utcnow()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.utcnow "(in Python v3.14)")
 can be problematic since it returns a naive UTC `datetime` object.

Quick example:

\# before
week\_ago \= datetime.datetime.utcnow() \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
if member.created\_at \> week\_ago:
    print(f'Member account {member} was created less than a week ago!')

\# after
\# The new helper function can be used here:
week\_ago \= discord.utils.utcnow() \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
\# ...or the equivalent result can be achieved with datetime.datetime.now():
week\_ago \= datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc) \- datetime.timedelta(days\=7)
if member.created\_at \> week\_ago:
    print(f'Member account {member} was created less than a week ago!')

The following have been changed from naive to aware [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects in UTC:

*   [`AuditLogEntry.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.created_at "discord.AuditLogEntry.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`BaseActivity.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.BaseActivity.created_at "discord.BaseActivity.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`ClientUser.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.ClientUser.created_at "discord.ClientUser.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`DMChannel.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.DMChannel.created_at "discord.DMChannel.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Emoji.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.created_at "discord.Emoji.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`GroupChannel.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.GroupChannel.created_at "discord.GroupChannel.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Guild.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.created_at "discord.Guild.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`abc.GuildChannel.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.GuildChannel.created_at "discord.abc.GuildChannel.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Invite.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Invite.created_at "discord.Invite.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Object.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Object.created_at "discord.Object.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Member.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Member.created_at "discord.Member.created_at")

     attribute
    
*   [`Role.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Role.created_at "discord.Role.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Spotify.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Spotify.created_at "discord.Spotify.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`Sticker.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Sticker.created_at "discord.Sticker.created_at")
     attribute
    
*   [`TeamMember.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TeamMember.created_at "discord.TeamMember.created_at")
     attribute

*   [`Webhook.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.created_at "discord.Webhook.created_at")

*   [`Template.updated_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Template.updated_at "discord.Template.updated_at")
     attribute
    
*   `timestamp` parameter in [`on_typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_typing "discord.on_typing")
     event
    
*   `last_pin` parameter in [`on_private_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_private_channel_pins_update "discord.on_private_channel_pins_update")
     event
    
*   `last_pin` parameter in [`on_guild_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update "discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update")
     event
    
*   Return type of [`utils.snowflake_time()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.snowflake_time "discord.utils.snowflake_time")
    

The following now accept aware [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 and assume that if the passed [`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 is naive, it is a local time:

*   [`abc.Messageable.history()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.history "discord.abc.Messageable.history")
     method
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guilds()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guilds "discord.Client.fetch_guilds")
     method
    
*   [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
     method
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_members "discord.Guild.fetch_members")
     method
    
*   [`TextChannel.purge()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.purge "discord.TextChannel.purge")
     method
    
*   [`Embed`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Embed "discord.Embed")
     constructor
    
*   [`Embed.timestamp`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Embed.timestamp "discord.Embed.timestamp")
     property setter
    
*   [`utils.sleep_until()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.sleep_until "discord.utils.sleep_until")
     function
    
*   [`utils.time_snowflake()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.time_snowflake "discord.utils.time_snowflake")
     function
    

Currently, there’s only one place in this library that doesn’t accept naive [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "(in Python v3.14)")
 objects:

*   `timed_out_until` parameter in [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
    > This has been done to prevent users from mistakenly applying incorrect timeouts to guild members.
    

Major Webhook Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#major-webhook-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Webhook support has been rewritten to work better with typings and rate limits.

As a result, synchronous functionality has been split to separate classes.

Quick example for asynchronous webhooks:

\# before
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    webhook \= discord.Webhook.from\_url('url-here', adapter\=discord.AsyncWebhookAdapter(session))
    await webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

\# after
async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
    webhook \= discord.Webhook.from\_url('url-here', session\=session)
    await webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

Quick example for synchronous webhooks:

\# before
webhook \= discord.Webhook.partial(123456, 'token-here', adapter\=discord.RequestsWebhookAdapter())
webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

\# after
webhook \= discord.SyncWebhook.partial(123456, 'token-here')
webhook.send('Hello World', username\='Foo')

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   Synchronous functionality of [`Webhook`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook "discord.Webhook")
     and [`WebhookMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage "discord.WebhookMessage")
     has been split to [`SyncWebhook`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.SyncWebhook "discord.SyncWebhook")
     and [`SyncWebhookMessage`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.SyncWebhookMessage "discord.SyncWebhookMessage")
    .
    
*   `WebhookAdapter` class has been removed and the interfaces based on it (`AsyncWebhookAdapter` and `RequestsWebhookAdapter`) are now considered implementation detail and should not be depended on.
    
*   `execute` alias for [`Webhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.send "discord.Webhook.send")
    /[`SyncWebhook.send()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.SyncWebhook.send "discord.SyncWebhook.send")
     has been removed.
    

Asset Redesign and Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#asset-redesign-and-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 object now encompasses all of the methods and attributes related to a CDN asset.

This means that all models with asset-related attribute and methods have been transformed to use this new design. As an example, here’s how these changes look for [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
 (of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 type):

*   `Guild.icon` (of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
     type) has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    .
    
*   `Guild.is_icon_animated` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.is_animated`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.is_animated "discord.Asset.is_animated")
    .
    
*   `Guild.icon_url` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
    .
    
*   `Guild.icon_url_as` has been replaced with [`Guild.icon.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    .
    
    > *   Helper methods [`Asset.with_size()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.with_size "discord.Asset.with_size")
    >     , [`Asset.with_format()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.with_format "discord.Asset.with_format")
    >     , and [`Asset.with_static_format()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.with_static_format "discord.Asset.with_static_format")
    >      have also been added.
    >     
    

In addition to this, [`Emoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji "discord.Emoji")
 and [`PartialEmoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.PartialEmoji "discord.PartialEmoji")
 now also share an interface similar to [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
’s:

*   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
     is now of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
     type.
    
*   `Emoji.url_as` has been removed.
    
*   `Emoji.url.read` has been replaced with [`Emoji.read()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.read "discord.Emoji.read")
    .
    
*   `Emoji.url.save` has been replaced with [`Emoji.save()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.save "discord.Emoji.save")
    .
    

[`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
 now always represent an actually existing CDN asset. This means that:

*   `str(x)` on an [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can no longer return an empty string.
    
*   `bool(x)` on an [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can no longer return `False`.
    
*   Attributes containing an optional [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
     can now be `None`.
    

The following were affected by this change:

*   [`AuditLogDiff`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff "discord.AuditLogDiff")
    
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.avatar "discord.AuditLogDiff.avatar")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.icon "discord.AuditLogDiff.icon")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   [`AuditLogDiff.splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.splash "discord.AuditLogDiff.splash")
    >      is now of [`Asset`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset "discord.Asset")
    >      type.
    >     
    
*   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
    
    > *   [`Emoji.url`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.url "discord.Emoji.url")
    >      is now of [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.14)")
    >      type.
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url_as` has been removed.
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url.read` (replaced by [`Emoji.read()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.read "discord.Emoji.read")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Emoji.url.save` (replaced by [`Emoji.save()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.save "discord.Emoji.save")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`GroupChannel.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.GroupChannel.icon "discord.GroupChannel.icon")
    
    > *   `GroupChannel.icon` (replaced by [`GroupChannel.icon.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `GroupChannel.icon_url` (replaced by [`GroupChannel.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.GroupChannel.icon "discord.GroupChannel.icon")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `GroupChannel.icon_url_as` (replaced by [`GroupChannel.icon.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Guild.banner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.banner "discord.Guild.banner")
    
    > *   `Guild.banner` (replaced by [`Guild.banner.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Guild.banner_url` (replaced by [`Guild.banner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.banner "discord.Guild.banner")
    >     )
    >

    >     >

    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Guild.discovery_splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.discovery_splash "discord.Guild.discovery_splash")

    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Guild.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.icon "discord.Guild.icon")
