    >     )
    >     
    > *   `PartialInviteGuild.splash_url` (replaced by [`PartialInviteGuild.splash`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.PartialInviteGuild.splash "discord.PartialInviteGuild.splash")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `PartialInviteGuild.splash_url_as` (replaced by [`PartialInviteGuild.splash.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Team.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Team.icon "discord.Team.icon")
    
    > *   `Team.icon` (replaced by [`Team.icon.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Team.icon_url` (replaced by [`Team.icon`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Team.icon "discord.Team.icon")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `Team.icon_url_as` (replaced by [`Team.icon.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`User.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.User.avatar "discord.User.avatar")
    
    > *   `User.avatar` (replaced by [`User.avatar.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `User.is_avatar_animated` (replaced by [`User.avatar.is_animated`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.is_animated "discord.Asset.is_animated")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `User.avatar_url` (replaced by [`User.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.User.avatar "discord.User.avatar")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `User.avatar_url_as` (replaced by [`User.avatar.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`User.default_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.User.default_avatar "discord.User.default_avatar")
    
    > *   `User.default_avatar` (replaced by [`User.default_avatar.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `User.default_avatar_url` (replaced by [`User.default_avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.User.default_avatar "discord.User.default_avatar")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `User.default_avatar_url_as` (replaced by [`User.default_avatar.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    
*   [`Webhook.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.avatar "discord.Webhook.avatar")
    
    > *   `Webhook.avatar` (replaced by [`Webhook.avatar.key`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.key "discord.Asset.key")
    >     )
    >     
    > *   `Webhook.avatar_url` (replaced by [`Webhook.avatar`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.avatar "discord.Webhook.avatar")
    >     )
    >     
    >     > *   The new attribute may now be `None`.
    >     >     
    >     
    > *   `Webhook.avatar_url_as` (replaced by [`Webhook.avatar.replace`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Asset.replace "discord.Asset.replace")
    >     )
    >     
    

Thread Support[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#thread-support "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

v2.0 has been updated to use a newer API gateway version which supports threads and as a result of this had to make few breaking changes. Most notably messages sent in guilds can, in addition to a [`TextChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel "discord.TextChannel")
, be sent in a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
.

The main differences between text channels and threads are:

*   Threads do not have their own permissions, they inherit the permissions of their parent channel.
    
    > *   This means that threads do not have these attributes:
    >     
    >     > *   `changed_roles`
    >     >     
    >     > *   `overwrites`
    >     >     
    >     > *   `permissions_synced`
    >     >     
    >     
    > 
    > Note
    > 
    > Text channels have a few dedicated permissions for threads:
    > 
    > *   [`Permissions.manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.create_public_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.create_public_threads "discord.Permissions.create_public_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.create_private_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.create_private_threads "discord.Permissions.create_private_threads")
    >     
    > *   [`Permissions.send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own NSFW status, they inherit it from their parent channel.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have an `nsfw` attribute.
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own topic.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have a `topic` attribute.
    >     
    
*   Threads do not have their own position in the channel list.
    
    > *   This means that [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    >      does not have a `position` attribute.
    >     
    
*   [`Thread.created_at`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.created_at "discord.Thread.created_at")
     of threads created before 10 January 2022 is `None`.
    
*   [`Thread.members`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.members "discord.Thread.members")
     is of type List\[[`ThreadMember`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.ThreadMember "discord.ThreadMember")\
    \] rather than List\[[`Member`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Member "discord.Member")\
    \]
    
    > *   Most of the time, this data is not provided and a call to [`Thread.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.fetch_members "discord.Thread.fetch_members")
    >      to fetch the partial objects is needed.
    >     
    

For convenience, [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
 has a set of properties and methods that return the information about the parent channel:

*   [`Thread.category`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.category "discord.Thread.category")
    
*   [`Thread.category_id`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.category_id "discord.Thread.category_id")
    
*   [`Thread.is_news()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.is_news "discord.Thread.is_news")
    
*   [`Thread.is_nsfw()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.is_nsfw "discord.Thread.is_nsfw")
    
*   [`Thread.permissions_for()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.permissions_for "discord.Thread.permissions_for")
    
    > *   Note that this outputs the permissions of the parent channel and you might need to check for different permissions when trying to determine if a member can do something.
    >     
    >     Here are some notable examples:
    >     
    >     *   A guild member can send messages in a text channel if they have [`send_messages`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages "discord.Permissions.send_messages")
    >          permission in it.
    >         
    >         A guild member can send messages in a public thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             .
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can send messages in a private thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`send_messages_in_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads "discord.Permissions.send_messages_in_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They’re either already a member of the thread or have a [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             .
    >             
    >         
    >     *   A guild member can edit a text channel if they have [`manage_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_channels "discord.Permissions.manage_channels")
    >          permission in it.
    >         
    >         A guild member can edit a thread if they have [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >          permission in its parent channel.
    >         
    >         Note
    >         
    >         A thread’s [`owner`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.owner "discord.Thread.owner")
    >          can archive a (not-locked) thread and edit its [`name`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.name "discord.Thread.name")
    >          and [`auto_archive_duration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.auto_archive_duration "discord.Thread.auto_archive_duration")
    >          without [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >          permission.
    >         
    >     *   A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a text channel if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in it.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in it or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a public thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in its parent channel or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`archived`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.archived "discord.Thread.archived")
    >             . Note that the guild member can unarchive a thread (if it’s not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             ) to react to a message.
    >             
    >         
    >         A guild member can react with an emoji to messages in a private thread if:
    >         
    >         *   They have [`read_message_history`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.read_message_history "discord.Permissions.read_message_history")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   They have [`add_reactions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.add_reactions "discord.Permissions.add_reactions")
    >              permission in its parent channel or the message already has that emoji reaction.
    >             
    >         *   They’re either already a member of the thread or have a [`manage_threads`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Permissions.manage_threads "discord.Permissions.manage_threads")
    >              permission in its parent channel.
    >             
    >         *   The thread is not [`archived`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.archived "discord.Thread.archived")
    >             . Note that the guild member can unarchive a thread (if it’s not [`locked`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread.locked "discord.Thread.locked")
    >             ) to react to a message.
    >             
    >         
    

The following changes have been made:

*   [`Message.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.channel "discord.Message.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Message.channel_mentions`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.channel_mentions "discord.Message.channel_mentions")
     list may now contain a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`AuditLogEntry.target`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogEntry.target "discord.AuditLogEntry.target")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`PartialMessage.channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.PartialMessage.channel "discord.PartialMessage.channel")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.get_channel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel "discord.Guild.get_channel")
     does not return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    s.
    
    > *   If you’re looking to get a channel or thread, use [`Guild.get_channel_or_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_channel_or_thread "discord.Guild.get_channel_or_thread")
    >      instead.
    >     
    > *   If you’re only looking to get threads, use [`Guild.get_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.get_thread "discord.Guild.get_thread")
    >      or [`TextChannel.get_thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.get_thread "discord.TextChannel.get_thread")
    >      instead.
    >     
    
*   `channel` parameter in [`on_guild_channel_pins_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update "discord.on_guild_channel_pins_update")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   `channel` parameter in [`on_typing()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_typing "discord.on_typing")
     may now be a [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Client.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_channel "discord.Client.fetch_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Client.get_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_channel "discord.Client.get_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_channel "discord.Guild.fetch_channel")
     may now return [`Thread`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Thread "discord.Thread")
    .
    

Removing In-Place Edits[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#removing-in-place-edits "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Most of the model methods that previously edited the model in-place have been updated to no longer do this. Instead, these methods will now return a new instance of the newly updated model. This has been done to avoid the library running into race conditions between in-place edits and gateway events on model updates. See [GH-4098](https://github.com/dolfies/discord.py-self/issues/4098)
 for more information.

Quick example:

\# before
await member.edit(nick\='new nick')
await member.send(f'Your new nick is {member.nick}')

\# after
updated\_member \= await member.edit(nick\='new nick')
await member.send(f'Your new nick is {updated\_member.nick}')

The following have been changed:

*   [`CategoryChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel.edit "discord.CategoryChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`CategoryChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel "discord.CategoryChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`Member.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Member.edit "discord.Member.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method only returns the updated [`Member`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Member "discord.Member")
    >      when certain fields are updated.
    >     
    
*   [`StageChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.edit "discord.StageChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`StageChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel "discord.StageChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`TextChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.edit "discord.TextChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`TextChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel "discord.TextChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`VoiceChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel.edit "discord.VoiceChannel.edit")
    
    > *   Note that this method will return `None` instead of [`VoiceChannel`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.VoiceChannel "discord.VoiceChannel")
    >      if the edit was only positional.
    >     
    
*   [`ClientUser.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.ClientUser.edit "discord.ClientUser.edit")
    
*   [`Emoji.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Emoji.edit "discord.Emoji.edit")
    
*   [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
    
*   [`Message.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.edit "discord.Message.edit")
    
*   [`Role.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Role.edit "discord.Role.edit")
    
*   [`Template.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Template.edit "discord.Template.edit")
    
*   [`Template.sync()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Template.sync "discord.Template.sync")
    
*   [`Webhook.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.edit "discord.Webhook.edit")
    
*   [`Webhook.edit_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.edit_message "discord.Webhook.edit_message")
    
*   [`WebhookMessage.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage.edit "discord.WebhookMessage.edit")
    

Sticker Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#sticker-changes "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Discord has changed how their stickers work and as such, sticker support has been reworked.

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   Type of [`Message.stickers`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.stickers "discord.Message.stickers")
     changed to List\[[`StickerItem`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerItem "discord.StickerItem")\
    \].
    
    > *   To get the [`Sticker`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Sticker "discord.Sticker")
    >      from [`StickerItem`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerItem "discord.StickerItem")
    >     , use [`StickerItem.fetch()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerItem.fetch "discord.StickerItem.fetch")
    >      or (only for stickers from guilds the bot is in) [`Client.get_sticker()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.get_sticker "discord.Client.get_sticker")
    >     .
    >     
    
*   [`Sticker.format`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Sticker.format "discord.Sticker.format")
     is now of [`StickerFormatType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerFormatType "discord.StickerFormatType")
     type.
    
*   `Sticker.tags` has been removed.
    
    > *   Depending on type of the sticker, [`StandardSticker.tags`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StandardSticker.tags "discord.StandardSticker.tags")
    >      or [`GuildSticker.emoji`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.GuildSticker.emoji "discord.GuildSticker.emoji")
    >      can be used instead.
    >     
    
*   `Sticker.image` and related methods have been removed.
    
*   `Sticker.preview_image` and related methods have been removed.
    
*   [`AuditLogDiff.type`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.AuditLogDiff.type "discord.AuditLogDiff.type")
     is now of Union\[[`ChannelType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.ChannelType "discord.ChannelType")\
    , [`StickerType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerType "discord.StickerType")\
    \] type.
    
*   The old `StickerType` enum has been renamed to [`StickerFormatType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerFormatType "discord.StickerFormatType")
    .
    
    > *   [`StickerType`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StickerType "discord.StickerType")
    >      now refers to a sticker type (official sticker vs guild-uploaded sticker) rather than its format type.
    >     
    

Integrations Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#integrations-changes "Permalink to this headline")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To support the new integration types, integration support has been reworked.

The following breaking changes have been made:

*   The old `Integration` class has been renamed to [`StreamIntegration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StreamIntegration "discord.StreamIntegration")
    .
    
*   [`Guild.integrations()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.integrations "discord.Guild.integrations")
     now returns subclasses of the new [`Integration`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Integration "discord.Integration")
     class.
    

Presence Updates Now Have A Separate Event[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#presence-updates-now-have-a-separate-event "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Presence updates (changes in member’s status and activity) now have a separate [`on_presence_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_presence_update "discord.on_presence_update")
 event. [`on_member_update()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.on_member_update "discord.on_member_update")
 event is now only called on member updates (changes in nickname, role, pending status, etc.).

From API perspective, these are separate events and as such, this change improves library’s consistency with the API. Presence updates usually are 90% of all handled events so splitting these should benefit listeners that were only interested in member updates.

Note that this event is also called when a [`Relationship`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Relationship "discord.Relationship")
 presence is updated.

Quick example:

\# before
@client.event
async def on\_member\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.nick != after.nick:
        await nick\_changed(before, after)
    if before.status != after.status:
        await status\_changed(before, after)

\# after
@client.event
async def on\_member\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.nick != after.nick:
        await nick\_changed(before, after)

@client.event
async def on\_presence\_update(self, before, after):
    if before.status != after.status:
        await status\_changed(before, after)

Moving Away From Custom AsyncIterator[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#moving-away-from-custom-asynciterator "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Asynchronous iterators in v1.0 were implemented using a special class named `AsyncIterator`. v2.0 instead provides regular asynchronous iterators with no added utility methods.

This means that usage of the following utility methods is no longer possible:

*   `AsyncIterator.next()`
    
    > Usage of an explicit `async for` loop should generally be preferred:
    > 
    > \# before
    > it \= channel.history()
    > while True:
    >     try:
    >         message \= await self.next()
    >     except discord.NoMoreItems:
    >         break
    >     print(f'Found message with ID {message.id}')
    > 
    > \# after
    > async for message in channel.history():
    >     print(f'Found message with ID {message.id}')
    > 
    > If you need to get next item from an iterator without a loop, you can use [`anext()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#anext "(in Python v3.14)")
    >  (new in Python 3.10) or [`__anext__()`](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__anext__ "(in Python v3.14)")
    >  instead:
    > 
    > \# before
    > it \= channel.history()
    > first \= await it.next()
    > if first.content \== 'do not iterate':
    >     return
    > async for message in it:
    >     ...
    > 
    > \# after
    > it \= channel.history()
    > first \= await anext(it)  \# await it.\_\_anext\_\_() on Python<3.10
    > if first.content \== 'do not iterate':
    >     return
    > async for message in it:
    >     ...
    
*   `AsyncIterator.get()`
    
    > \# before
    > msg \= await channel.history().get(author\_\_name\='Dave')
    > 
    > \# after
    > msg \= await discord.utils.get(channel.history(), author\_\_name\='Dave')
    
*   `AsyncIterator.find()`
    
    > def predicate(event):
    >     return event.reason is not None
    > 
    > \# before
    > event \= await guild.audit\_logs().find(predicate)
    > 
    > \# after
    > event \= await discord.utils.find(predicate, guild.audit\_logs())
    
*   `AsyncIterator.flatten()`
    
    > \# before
    > users \= await reaction.users().flatten()
    > 
    > \# after
    > users \= \[user async for user in reaction.users()\]
    
*   `AsyncIterator.chunk()`
    
    > \# before
    > async for leader, \*users in reaction.users().chunk(3):
    >     ...
    > 
    > \# after
    > async for leader, \*users in discord.utils.as\_chunks(reaction.users(), 3):
    >     ...
    
*   `AsyncIterator.map()`
    
    > \# before
    > content\_of\_messages \= \[\]
    > async for content in channel.history().map(lambda m: m.content):
    >     content\_of\_messages.append(content)
    > 
    > \# after
    > content\_of\_messages \= \[message.content async for message in channel.history()\]
    
*   `AsyncIterator.filter()`
    
    > def predicate(message):
    >     return not message.author.bot
    > 
    > \# before
    > user\_messages \= \[\]
    > async for message in channel.history().filter(lambda m: not m.author.bot):
    >     user\_messages.append(message)
    > 
    > \# after
    > user\_messages \= \[message async for message in channel.history() if not m.author.bot\]
    

To ease this transition, these changes have been made:

*   Added [`utils.as_chunks()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.as_chunks "discord.utils.as_chunks")
     as an alternative for `AsyncIter.chunk`.
    
*   Added support for [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
     to [`utils.find()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.find "discord.utils.find")
    .
    
*   Added support for [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
     to [`utils.get()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.utils.get "discord.utils.get")
    .
    

The return type of the following methods has been changed to an [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
:

*   [`abc.Messageable.history()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.abc.Messageable.history "discord.abc.Messageable.history")
    
*   [`Client.fetch_guilds()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.fetch_guilds "discord.Client.fetch_guilds")
    
*   [`Guild.audit_logs()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.audit_logs "discord.Guild.audit_logs")
    
*   [`Guild.fetch_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.fetch_members "discord.Guild.fetch_members")
    
*   [`Reaction.users()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Reaction.users "discord.Reaction.users")
    

The `NoMoreItems` exception was removed as calling [`anext()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#anext "(in Python v3.14)")
 or [`__anext__()`](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#object.__anext__ "(in Python v3.14)")
 on an [asynchronous iterator](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-asynchronous-iterator "(in Python v3.14)")
 will now raise [`StopAsyncIteration`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#StopAsyncIteration "(in Python v3.14)")
.

Changing certain lists to be lazy sequences instead[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#changing-certain-lists-to-be-lazy-sequences-instead "Permalink to this headline")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order to improve performance when calculating the length of certain lists, certain attributes were changed to return a sequence rather than a [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
.

A sequence is similar to a [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
 except it is read-only. In order to get a list again you can call [`list`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#list "(in Python v3.14)")
 on the resulting sequence.

The following properties were changed to return a sequence instead of a list:

*   [`Client.guilds`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.guilds "discord.Client.guilds")
    
*   [`Client.emojis`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.emojis "discord.Client.emojis")
    
*   [`Client.private_channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.private_channels "discord.Client.private_channels")
    
*   [`Guild.roles`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.roles "discord.Guild.roles")
    
*   [`Guild.channels`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.channels "discord.Guild.channels")
    
*   [`Guild.members`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.members "discord.Guild.members")
    

This change should be transparent, unless you are modifying the sequence by doing things such as `list.append`.

Embed Changes[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#embed-changes "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Originally, embeds used a special sentinel to denote emptiness or remove an attribute from display. The `Embed.Empty` sentinel was made when Discord’s embed design was in a nebulous state of flux. Since then, the embed design has stabilised and thus the sentinel is seen as legacy.

Therefore, `Embed.Empty` has been removed in favour of `None`.

Additionally, `Embed.__eq__` has been implemented thus embeds becoming unhashable (e.g. using them in sets or dict keys).

\# before
embed \= discord.Embed(title\='foo')
embed.title \= discord.Embed.Empty
embed \== embed.copy() \# False

\# after
embed \= discord.Embed(title\='foo')
embed.title \= None
embed \== embed.copy() \# True
{embed, embed} \# Raises TypeError

Removal of `InvalidArgument` Exception[¶](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/migrating.html#removal-of-invalidargument-exception "Permalink to this headline")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The custom `InvalidArgument` exception has been removed and functions and methods that raised it are now raising [`TypeError`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#TypeError "(in Python v3.14)")
 and/or [`ValueError`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError "(in Python v3.14)")
 instead.

The following methods have been changed:

*   [`Message.add_reaction()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.add_reaction "discord.Message.add_reaction")
    
*   `AutoShardedClient.change_presence()`
    
*   [`Client.change_presence()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.change_presence "discord.Client.change_presence")
    
*   [`Reaction.clear()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Reaction.clear "discord.Reaction.clear")
    
*   [`Message.clear_reaction()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.clear_reaction "discord.Message.clear_reaction")
    
*   [`Guild.create_category()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_category "discord.Guild.create_category")
    
*   [`Guild.create_custom_emoji()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji "discord.Guild.create_custom_emoji")
    
*   [`Client.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Client.create_guild "discord.Client.create_guild")
    
*   [`Template.create_guild()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Template.create_guild "discord.Template.create_guild")
    
*   [`StageChannel.create_instance()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.StageChannel.create_instance "discord.StageChannel.create_instance")
    
*   [`Guild.create_role()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_role "discord.Guild.create_role")
    
*   [`Guild.create_stage_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_stage_channel "discord.Guild.create_stage_channel")
    
*   [`Guild.create_text_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_text_channel "discord.Guild.create_text_channel")
    
*   [`Guild.create_voice_channel()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.create_voice_channel "discord.Guild.create_voice_channel")
    
*   [`TextChannel.create_webhook()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.create_webhook "discord.TextChannel.create_webhook")
    
*   [`Webhook.delete()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.delete "discord.Webhook.delete")
    
*   [`WebhookMessage.delete()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.WebhookMessage.delete "discord.WebhookMessage.delete")
    
*   [`Webhook.delete_message()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Webhook.delete_message "discord.Webhook.delete_message")
    
*   [`CategoryChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.CategoryChannel.edit "discord.CategoryChannel.edit")
    
*   [`ClientUser.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.ClientUser.edit "discord.ClientUser.edit")
    
*   [`Guild.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.edit "discord.Guild.edit")
    
*   [`Message.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Message.edit "discord.Message.edit")
    
*   [`Role.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Role.edit "discord.Role.edit")

*   [`TextChannel.edit()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.TextChannel.edit "discord.TextChannel.edit")

*   [`Guild.estimate_pruned_members()`](https://discordpy-self.readthedocs.io/en/v2.1.0/api.html#discord.Guild.estimate_pruned_members "discord.Guild.estimate_pruned_members")
